closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/buildi.content:
# Step 1: Build a Dialled-In ICP # **Build a Dialled-In ICP** ---
🚨 **QUICK NOTE:** This is the most important section within our system. I’m walking you through this because **everyone** *thinks* they have a clear ICP. Almost nodbody does. This fixes that. If you’re sending campaigns and not booking, there’s a 90% chance this is the root cause. This section is the foundation. Because if your ICP is off, your copy sucks, your offer feels vague, and you waste months wondering why nobody bites.
## Before You Begin: Why This Page Matters More Than Anything
ℹ️ This framework took me years to create. Hundreds of client calls. Failed campaigns. Poor offers. Clients who churned and lost revenue.
What you’re about to read is the result of: - $100,000+ in coaching, consulting, and agency ops - 10+ years of working with every type of client under the sun - Real-world failures that nearly broke the business - Rebuilding around one question: **"Who can we predictably win with?"** I didn’t write this to sound smart. I wrote this so you could skip the years, stress and money it took me to figure it out. This is the single most valuable asset in this system. If you skip it, the rest won’t work. If you implement it, every other stage becomes easier, faster, and more profitable. You don’t need to make the same mistakes. You just need to use this as your filter. --- ## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll have: - ✅ A clear understanding of who you can actually get results for - ✅ A two-part ICP framework that works for any agency type - ✅ A qualification checklist that prevents bad-fit clients - ✅ The ability to charge premium prices with confidence This becomes your foundation. Everything else builds from here. --- ## Why Most People Get Stuck Here --- Here's what I see over and over again: **"We work with B2B SaaS companies."** That's not an ICP. That's a demographic guess. **"We target marketing agencies that need more leads."** Cool. There are 1M+ marketing agencies. You'll book nothing trying to speak to all of them. **"We help anyone who wants to grow."** Translation: no one replies, no one converts, and you're stuck guessing. ### The Real Problem Most people think ICP = buyer persona. They ask: *"Who might buy from me?"* **Wrong question.** The right question: *"Who can I actually get results for?"* Because **you can sign anyone. But you can only scale with clients you can predictably win with.** --- ## What an ICP Actually Is --- Your ICP isn't a marketing exercise. **It's a results filter. A checklist of who you can work with** It tells you exactly who you should work with and who you should avoid. ### Most People Think: - ICP = Demographics (company size, industry, revenue) - Purpose = Find people who might buy - Usage = Marketing and outreach only ### Reality: - **ICP = Who you can get results for consistently** - **Purpose = Prevent bad-fit clients from entering your business** - **Usage = Marketing, sales qualification, AND business strategy** When you get this right: - Every client you sign gets results - They stick longer and pay more - You build bulletproof case studies - You can charge premium prices - Referrals start flowing ![ChatGPT Image May 21, 2025, 09_49_34 AM.png](Step%201%20Build%20a%20Dialled-In%20ICP/ChatGPT_Image_May_21_2025_09_49_34_AM.png) --- ## The Two-Part ICP Framework --- Every scalable ICP has exactly **two parts**: ### Part 1: WHO can you get results for? - Company type + decision maker - Market requirements ### Part 2: WHAT do they need to have? - Internal capabilities required for success **Both parts must be dialled in, or your ICP doesn't work.** --- ## Part 1: WHO Can You Get Results For? --- This is about identifying the specific **company type** and **decision maker** where your method works best. ### The Company Type You need to get specific. Not "SaaS companies" but "B2B SaaS companies selling to enterprise." ### The Decision Maker Who actually buys your service? Not "marketing team" but "VP Marketing" or "Founder." ### Market Requirements This is the part most people miss. You need to check: **Does their target market work with your method?** **LeadGen Agency Example:** **Company Type:** CRM software companies **Decision Maker:** Founders **Market Requirement:** Target market of 40K+ prospects that are scrapable on Apollo **Why the market requirement matters:** If they sell to a tiny niche that isn't on Apollo, leadgen won't work no matter how good everything else is. --- ## Part 2: WHAT Do They Need to Have? --- This is about the **internal capabilities** your prospect needs for your method to work. These are non-negotiable requirements. If they don't have them, you can't get results. **LeadGen Agency Example:** **Required Capabilities:** - ✅ **Sellable offer** (so leads actually convert) - ✅ **3+ case studies** (for credibility in outreach) - ✅ **10%+ close rate** (so they convert the traffic you send) - ✅ **$20K+ customer LTV** (so ROI justifies lead cost) **Why each matters:** - **No sellable offer** = leads won't convert, they'll blame you - **No case studies** = no social proof, lower reply rates - **Low close rate** = they waste your leads, blame your quality - **Low LTV** = can't afford quality leads, want cheap volume ### Why Part 2 Matters Part 2 becomes your **qualification checklist**. Every discovery call: - Do they check every box? - **If yes** → you can get results → sign them - **If no** → you can't get results → don't sign them This prevents the client churn cycle that keeps most agencies stuck. --- ## ICP Action Plan --- ### Step 1: Identify Your WHO (Part 1) ### Step 2: Define Your WHAT (Part 2) ### Step 3: Write Your ICP Formula *"We help [Decision Maker] at [Company Type] who have [Part 2 Requirements] and need [Your Solution]."* **Example:** *"We help Founders of CRM software companies who have proven case studies, $20K+ LTV, and 10%+ close rates, but need a scalable outbound system to break through their inbound plateau."* ### Step 4: Test and Refine --- ## Real Example: Lead Gen Agency ICP
ℹ️ Let’s run it through the full ICP filter in practice:
### **ICP Narrative:** We help Founders of CRM software companies selling to SMBs, who have 3+ outcome-based case studies, $20K+ LTV, and a 10%+ close rate — but no outbound system that books 15–20 demos/month consistently. ### **Broken Down by the 5 ICP Filters:** 1. **Job Title + Responsibility is Clear?** → *Founder of a CRM software company* - signs deals, owns growth 2. **Can they buy now?** → Yes - they’ve hit a ceiling with inbound, feel urgency to scale 3. **Do you have proof for this ICP?** → Yes - booked 68 meetings for similar client in 90 days 4. **Can you find them in volume?** → Yes - 47,000 CRM platforms globally on Apollo 5. **Do they get fast results?** → Yes - if they have LTV $20K+, proven offer, and can close at 10%+ --- ## Why This Works --- - It’s built from real clients/experience and research - It filters for *both* surface-level traits and internal capabilities - It directly informs offer design, campaign copy, and list scraping - You can immediately qualify new leads against this or disqualify fast > If your ICP doesn’t let you say “yes” or “no” on a sales call in 60 seconds, it’s not an ICP, it’s a guess. > ← Previous [Stage 1: Building Your GTM Strategy](/resources/building-your-gtm-strategy-142f6d94771f81baa749f6a0e188a76b) Next → [Step 2: Buyer Persona Builder](/resources/step-2-buyer-persona-builder-219f6d94771f80eaad16eaf50dbfd389) On this page

# Step 2: Buyer Persona Builder # Step 2: Buyer Persona Builder ---
🚨 **QUICK NOTE:** Your ICP told you WHO you can get results for. Your buyer persona and this page shows you HOW to speak to them so they actually respond. Most campaigns fail here They know who to target But write emails talking to companies Not humans
ℹ️ Stop writing to job titles. Start writing to the person behind the title.
## Building on Your ICP --- You've just identified who you can get results for using the two-part ICP framework: **Part 1:** CRM software founders (WHO) **Part 2:** With case studies, $20K+ LTV, 10%+ close rate (WHAT they need to have)
ℹ️ But here's what most people miss: **Knowing WHO to target doesn't mean you know HOW to talk to them.**
**Example:** - **Bad messaging:** "Hi John, I help CRM companies get more leads" - **Good messaging:** "Hi John, saw your post about inbound plateauing. Most CRM founders hit this wall around $2M ARR. Here's how [competitor] broke through..." **The difference?** The second one speaks to their actual world, not just their job title. --- ## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this section, you'll have: ✅ A simple framework to understand how your buyer thinks ✅ Real insights you can use in all of your messaging ✅ Campaign hooks that actually get replies ✅ A full example you can copy, test, and tweak --- ## Why Most Personas Are Useless --- Here's what I see constantly: *"Marketing agency owners who want more leads and care about ROI."* **This is useless because:** - It could describe 500,000+ people - Doesn't tell you how they think or feel - Gives you no messaging direction - Sounds like everyone else **The result:** Generic emails that get ignored. --- ![buyer_persona_diagram.png](Step%202%20Buyer%20Persona%20Builder/buyer_persona_diagram.png) ## **Bad Persona vs. Great Persona** --- | **Bad Persona** | **Good Persona** | | --- | --- | | "CMO who wants more leads" | Jake: CMO at $5M SaaS ROAS tanked, inbound dried up, CEO asking why pipeline is flat Has 90 days to fix it or lose budget | | “Founder of CRM software” | Laura: Founder at $3M CRM platform. Stuck at same MRR for 6 months, reliant upon inbound and referrals and can’t scale the business because of it. Struggles to communicate value of their CRM. Tried 2 agencies before, neither could get results. | # How To Create Your Buyer Persona --- ## The MIRROR Framework --- This is the framework I created to help you solve for this. Any time you need to create a buyer persona, you can come straight back to this framework.
ℹ️ Each step gives you a specific lens into how your buyer thinks, feels, speaks, and acts so you can write like you're inside their head.
| **Step** | **Name** | **What to Uncover** | | --- | --- | --- | | **M** | **Map the Role** | What are they responsible for? What does their workday actually look like? What pressures do they have? | | **I** | **Identify Goals + Frictions** | What are they trying to achieve? Where are they stuck? What's slowing them down? | | **R** | **Reveal Their Current Reality** | What have they tried? What’s working vs. not? What tools, vendors, or strategies? | | **R** | **Recognise the Stakes** | What happens if they don’t fix this? What's at risk personally, financially, reputationally? | | **O** | **Observe Their Words** | What language do they use in Slack, DMs, sales calls, Reddit, or LinkedIn? | | **R** | **Reflect It Back** | Turn it all into a Messaging Snapshot that shapes hooks, openers, CTAs, and sales call angles. | This is your buyer's **job, context, pressure, goals, and worldview** not just their pain. Once this is filled, you won’t just have a persona. You’ll have a roadmap to write copy that gets replies, wins trust, and moves deals forward. --- ## Where to Find the Inputs --- ### Internal Sources (If You’ve Had Clients) - **Sales Calls** → Look for stress, hesitation, specific language - **Slack / DMs** → Pay attention to venting, unmet expectations, or the thing you helped with most - **Email Replies** → Scan for frustrations and real objections - **Onboarding Forms** → Especially questions like “What didn’t work before?” - **Cancellation Notes** → This is what future buyers are afraid of ### External Sources (If You Haven’t Had Clients Yet) - **LinkedIn Comments** → Raw emotion, real opinions, industry pressure - **Podcasts** → Listen to founders/execs share what’s hard, what failed - **Industry Webinars** → How they talk when peers are watching - **Subreddits** → Anonymous venting = honest gold (e.g. r/sales, r/startups) - **YouTube Interviews** → Watch for beliefs, doubts, personal stakes ### ChatGPT Hack Once you’ve gathered insights, paste them into ChatGPT with: > “Summarise the emotional + strategic profile of this persona using MIRROR. Include: > > - Core role and responsibilities > - Goals and frustrations > - What they’ve tried > - Risks they face > - Language and emotional tone” --- ## Buyer Awareness: The Final Filter
ℹ️ Most founders write copy that’s “kinda close” but not close enough. They’ve got the right ICP. The right persona. But they’re saying the right thing… **at the wrong time.** This is where buyer awareness comes in.
### Your buyer is somewhere on the decision spectrum: - Some don’t even know they have a problem - Some know the problem but haven’t looked for solutions - Some are comparing options right now - Some just need a reason to say yes **Your message needs to meet them where they are, not where you wish they were.** --- ## The 4 Awareness Stages
ℹ️ Every buyer is in one of these four buckets:
| **Stage** | **What They Know** | **What They Need to Hear First** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Unaware** | Don’t even know there’s a problem | Insight, pattern interrupt, new perspective | | **Problem Aware** | Know something’s broken, unsure why | Diagnosis, name the pain, give clarity | | **Solution Aware** | Actively looking at options | Proof, case studies, mechanism | | **Most Aware** | Ready to buy, comparing final options | Offer, differentiation, urgency | --- ## Matching Message to Awareness | **Stage** | **Lead With** | **CTA Style** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Unaware** | New insight or surprising pattern | Curiosity (“Is this a priority for you?”) | | **Problem Aware** | Clear diagnosis of what’s not working | Exploration (“Want to see how others solved this?”) | | **Solution Aware** | Proof your method works | Direct CTA (“Here’s what it did for X, want in?”) | | **Most Aware** | Strong offer with urgency or risk reversal | Buy-now push (“Here’s the plan”) | ## How to Use This With Your Persona Once you’ve built your MIRROR snapshot ask: > “What awareness stage is this person likely in when they see my message?” >
ℹ️ If they don’t yet see the problem → Don’t sell the solution. If they’ve tried 3 things already → Don’t open with education. If they’re shopping for answers → Lead with your edge.
👉 This is how you stop guessing what to say and start saying the exact thing that gets them to act. ## ✍️ Translate Your Persona Into Messaging ---
ℹ️ You’ve mapped out your persona. You know what they’re thinking, where they’re stuck, their awareness level and what they’ve already tried.
## Here’s how to turn that into messaging that lands: --- ### Step 1: Pull 5 Key Insights 1. What are they trying to achieve? 2. What’s in their way? 3. What have they already tried? 4. What are they under pressure to fix? 5. What awareness stage are they in? That’s your messaging structure. --- ### Step 2: Choose a Message Angle Each message should do **one** of the following: - **Echo their frustration** “Tried 3 agencies. Same garbage leads.” - **Call out a pressure** “CEO’s asking where Q3 pipeline is coming from.” - **Make the invisible problem obvious** “Lead quality isn’t the problem. It’s how you’re qualifying.” - **Point to a new mechanism** “Built-for-CRM outbound system → 87 demos in 90 days.” --- ### Step 3: Plug It Into Real Copy --- ### 👤 Example Persona (Problem-Aware) **Jake** Founder of $3M CRM platform Stuck at same MRR 6+ months. Inbound has stagnated. Tried 2 agencies, both overpromised, underdelivered. Wants growth. Can’t afford sales team yet. --- ### ✉️ Example Cold Email > **Subject:** > > > {[FirstName}} inbound dried up? > > **Body:** > > Hi Jake, > > Most founders of CRM software companies hit a wall around $2M–$3M ARR where inbound leads start to stagnate, and every new “lead gen” agency sounds the same. > > We just helped another CRM founder book 87 qualified demos in 90 days after firing two agencies that overpromised and underdelivered. > > Want to see the outbound system that did it? > > Sean > --- **✅ Why it works:** - **Hooks the pain** → "inbound dries up" - **Echoes the past** → "fired two agencies" - **Injects credibility** → 87 demos in 90 days - **Fits awareness stage** → not teaching outbound, just pointing to a solution --- ## Reminder --- This framework took years to build. It’s what works in cold email, sales calls, and VSLs. Use it. ← Previous [Step 1: Build Your ICP](/resources/step-1-build-a-dialled-in-icp-219f6d94771f80988eabc94bc7dc9aeb) Next → [Step 3: Offers + Positioning](/resources/step-3-offers-positioning-219f6d94771f80bea5f6ef818dbd4d67) On this page

# Step 4: Strategic Messaging # **Step 4: Strategic Messaging**
**Make Your Whole Business Say “This Is for You”**
---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Your positioning isn’t just something you say on sales calls. It should show up everywhere: your site, email footer, LinkedIn bio, calendar invite. You’ve already done the hard part: ✅ Dialled in your ICP ✅ Created your buyer persona ✅ Built a killer offer ✅ Positioned it to feel premium Now it’s time to **plaster it everywhere.** Your job: Make sure every buyer touchpoint says the same thing — “This is built for me.”
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know what to say across all your buyer-facing assets ✅ Make sure your message sounds clear, consistent, and premium ✅ Use your Positioning Formula in websites, decks, emails, and bios ✅ Fix the biggest leaks in your messaging system ✅ Turn confused clicks into “this is exactly what I need” ## 1. Why Your Assets Need to Sell *Before* You Do ---
ℹ️ Most buyers won’t hear your pitch. They’ll skim your site. Glance at your CTA. Hover over your LinkedIn bio. That’s where the sale starts, or ends.
--- Right now, your message is **leaking.** Your offer’s strong. Your positioning is tight. But if your **assets don’t say it**, buyers don’t see it. --- ### The Hose Analogy: Imagine turning on a garden hose. Right now, your message is **spraying in all directions.** Your website says one thing. Your LinkedIn says another. Your cold emails say something else. There’s no pressure. No direction. No impact. --- What you want is a **pressure nozzle**: → **One focused message. Across every touchpoint.** --- ![ChatGPT Image Jun 20, 2025, 04_26_29 PM.png](Step%204%20Strategic%20Messaging/ChatGPT_Image_Jun_20_2025_04_26_29_PM.png) ### Why This Matters: | ❌ Weak Message | ✅ Strong Message | | --- | --- | | “Not sure what this is…” | “This is built for me” | | “Another cold email agency?” | “A system for CRM SaaS at $2–5M ARR” | | “Why is this $4K?” | “Ah — it’s a full outbound engine” | --- Buyers don’t read every word. They remember **the feeling** your message gives them. → “This sounds clear.” → “This looks serious.” → “This feels premium.” Let’s make sure that feeling shows up **everywhere.** ## 2. What the Buyer Actually Sees ---
ℹ️ You think they’re “researching you.” They’re not. They’re skimming 5 things and making a snap call: “Is this for me or not?”*
### **Here’s what a typical buyer journey looks like:** --- ### The Real Buyer Journey: 1. **Speed reads your email** > “We help SaaS founders get more demos.” > > > Nothing stands out. > 2. **Checks your LinkedIn** > Headline: “Founder | Cold Email Expert” > > > Vague. Generic. No clear result. > 3. **Checks your site** > “We do outbound and generate leads…” > > > They bounce after 9 seconds. > 4. **Checks your calendar link or email footer** > “Book a call” > > > With who? For what? Why now? > 5. **Books the call… skeptical** You now have to *recover lost trust* instead of starting ahead. --- ### The Restaurant Test Imagine you’re booking dinner for Friday night. You open the website and it says: > “We serve food.” > > > “Contact us to eat.” > No menu. No photos. Would you book? Of course not. That’s what your buyer sees when your messaging is scattered. --- ### ❌ vs ✅ Messaging Examples | **Touchpoint** | **Lazy Version** | **Strategic Version** | | --- | --- | --- | | LinkedIn Headline | “B2B Lead Gen | Founder of team that… | | Website Hero | “We generate qualified meetings” | “Add $30K MRR with a proven 90-day outbound engine, used by 87 CRM founders” | | VSL Opener | “We help founders get more leads” | “Here’s how we booked 87 demos in 90 days using our 3-part outbound system” | | Calendar Description | “Book a call to learn more” | “We’ll walk you through our 90-Day Engine to see if it’s a fit for your team” | --- Every one of these matters. The sale doesn’t start on the call, it starts when they notice you. Fix the message, and you close them *before* you pitch them. --- ## 3. Update These Assets Immediately ---
ℹ️  You’ve got your Positioning Formula. Now paste it everywhere.
### Here’s your **positioning-to-asset translation map**: | **Asset** | **What To Update** | | --- | --- | | Website Hero | Use your positioning statement | | LinkedIn Headline | Hook with outcome + system | | Cold Email CTA | Mirror offer + risk reversal | | Calendar Description | Reinforce positioning | | Deck Headline | Lead with the outcome | | Lead Magnet Title | Align with dream outcome | | VSL Intro | System + outcome + proof | | Email Signature | Add a 1-liner or system name | | Social Bios | Positioning in 1 sentence | --- ### **Audit your current assets.** Fix the ones that don’t align with your new GTM strategy. Your buyer should feel **zero friction** between any of your touchpoints. Same voice. Same result. Same system. --- ## 4. GTM Stage 1 Recap: ---
ℹ️ You’ve now built something most agency founders never do: A real go-to-market engine.
### **Here’s what you now have in place:** ✅ A clear ICP you can win with ✅ A buyer persona you can sell to ✅ A premium, outcome-driven offer ✅ Positioning that makes it feel strategic ✅ Messaging and assets that sell *before* you speak This is no longer “sending emails and hoping.” This is **building an asset that compounds.** --- ### What Happens Next: --- Now that you’ve built your GTM engine: → Sales gets easier → Calls feel different → Buyers show up already wanting in You don’t need to convince. You just need to confirm fit. ← Previous [Step 3: Offers + Positioning](/resources/step-3-offers-positioning-219f6d94771f80bea5f6ef818dbd4d67) Next → [Closing Clients $30K/Month Roadmap](/resources/closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap-9c64eebf4ffe4c818b040d6a47df372a) On this page

# Offers: Build a No-Brainer Offer Your ICP Can’t Ignore # Offers: Build a No-Brainer Offer Your ICP Can’t Ignore ---
🚨 **QUICK NOTE:** This is one of the most important pages in the system. It’s long, but for a reason. You’ve nailed who you’re targeting. Now we’re going to turn that into something they actually say yes to. Go through it. Do the prompts. By the end, you’ll have an offer that makes people say, “Wait… how do we start?”
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll have: ✅ A clear, de-risked offer your ICP actually wants ✅ A short offer statement you can drop into cold emails or sales calls ✅ A stack of assets that make your service feel premium ✅ A guarantee that kills objections before they show up ✅ A final test to make sure it all clicks in under 7 seconds ## 1: Your Service ≠ Your Offer ---
ℹ️ → Why “we do [X service]” is why you’re stuck at $5K/month Most people confuse their **service** with their **offer** A service is **NOT** an offer
Let’s clear this up immediately… ### **Your service** is the thing you *do.* ### **Your offer** is the reason someone *buys.* If you’re selling “cold email” “funnel builds,” “appointment setting,” or “content strategy” you’re selling the **mechanism**. But buyers don’t care about mechanisms. They buy **outcomes**, **shortcuts**, **proof**, and **safety**. --- ### What You Think You’re Selling vs. What They Actually Want | **You Say** | **They Hear** | | --- | --- | | “We do cold outreach” | “So do 10,000 other agencies” | | “We book meetings” | “Meetings with who? That actually close?” | | “We charge per call” | “Sounds risky and probably spammy” | | “We run paid ads” | “Cool… but do they convert to deals?” | | “We help you grow” | “Sound. So does every other slide deck on LinkedIn” | --- ### Why Most Offers Don’t Convert - ❌ Vague promise - ❌ No urgency - ❌ No differentiation - ❌ All risk is on the buyer > You’re losing deals because your offer isn’t **clear**, **fast**, or **believable** enough. > --- ### What Actually Makes an Offer Convert
ℹ️ “If you want to sell faster, remove the need to ‘think about it.’”
### **Use this base formula:**
👉 ### **Offer = Result + Shortcut + Proof + Risk Removal**
### Let’s break it down: - **🎯 Result** → What outcome are they buying? (Not “ads” or “calls” but calls, sign-ups, revenue) - **⏩ Shortcut** → How is this faster/easier than them doing it alone? - **🧠 Proof** → Who’s this worked for? Do you show it clearly? - **🛡️ Risk Removal** → What happens if it doesn’t work? (You lose, not them) --- ### Bad Offer > “We help SaaS companies book 20 meetings/month with decision-makers.” > - → Too broad - → No unique angle - → No proof - → No urgency - → Buyer carries all the risk --- ### Great Offer
👉 “We run the entire outbound operation for CRM founders at $2-5M ARR. We write the emails, build the lists, send the campaigns, and book the calls directly into your teams calendar. You just show up and close. We guarantee 25 qualified calls that show up, or we work for free.”
✅ Speaks directly to the ICP ✅ Shows outcome, shortcut, and proof ✅ Flips the risk ✅ Feels custom-built for them --- ### **📌 The goal of this section:**
ℹ️ Flip your mindset from **“what you do”** → to **“what they get.”** Because when your offer sounds like a no-brainer — objections disappear.
## 2: The $30K Offer Checklist
ℹ️ → The 5 questions that turn “we do cold email” into “holy sh*t, I need this.”
--- This checklist forces clarity. No vague phrases. No weak promises. Just answers that turn what you *do* into something they *buy immediately*. --- ### 📋 Use this checklist to stress-test your offer: | ❓ Question | 💡 Why It Matters | | --- | --- | | **1. Who is this for?** | If it’s not buyer specific, no one feels like it’s for them | | **2. What outcome do they want now?** | Anchors the offer to urgency + desire | | **3. What’s broken or annoying about how they’re trying now?** | Sells the gap between current state and desired state | | **4. What shortcut do you give?** | Show you understand them and how you can help | | **5. Why should they buy this now?** | Injects urgency, fear of inaction, or a clear switch to flip | --- ### Example Breakdown: Bad vs. Great Let’s walk through this using the same example service **cold email lead gen**. --- ### ❌ **BAD OFFER**
ℹ️ “We book 15–20 meetings/month with decision-makers.”
**Let’s break this down using the checklist:** | **Question** | **Answer** | | --- | --- | | Who is this for? | “B2B SaaS companies” → Too broad | | Desired Outcome? | “More meetings” → Generic | | Frustration? | Doesn’t show any | | Shortcut? | None - sounds like everyone else | | Urgency? | Zero reason to act now | ### 👎 **Verdict:** This offer is vague, undifferentiated, and forgettable. Even if it “works,” it doesn’t sell itself. --- ### ✅ **GREAT OFFER** (Built using this checklist)
👉 “We run the entire outbound operation for CRM founders at $2-5M ARR. We write the emails, build the lists, send the campaigns, and book the calls directly into your teams calendar. You just show up and close. We guarantee 25 qualified calls that show up, or we work for free.”
**Now plug it into the checklist:** | Question | Answer | | --- | --- | | Who is this for? | CRM founders at $2–5M ARR | | Desired Outcome? | Qualified calls without the stress/hassle | | Frustration? | Tried agencies, stuck with no consistent pipeline | | Shortcut? | Proven system, full service, everything managed | | Urgency? | Guarantee + proof | ✅ **Verdict:** Feels tailored, urgent, and de-risks the buy. This sounds like a system, not a service. And that’s why it closes. ---
ℹ️ **TIP:** Write your own answers to the checklist. If they’re vague, generic, or hard to answer → your offer’s still not ready. If each one is specific and obvious → you’re ready to build the actual statement.
--- ## 3: The Offer Creation Framework
ℹ️ → Build what your buyer already wants. Then say it so clearly they’d feel stupid saying no.
--- **Most founders guess their offer.** This framework removes the guesswork and forces you to build an offer *backward* from what your ICP already wants, hates, and is actively trying to fix. --- ### 📌 **REMINDER:** You’ve already done the hard work. ✅ You know your ICP ✅ You’ve built the persona ✅ You understand their fears, pressure, and urgency **Now let’s turn that into an offer that sells.** --- ### Step 1: Fill Out the 5 Core Offer Inputs Use the prompts below to define your offer’s raw ingredients. | 🔍 **Input** | 💡 **Prompt** | | --- | --- | | 🎯 **Target** | Who is this built for? *(e.g. “CRM founders at $2–5M ARR”)* | | 🥇 **Desired Outcome** | What result are they chasing right now? *(e.g. “predictable outbound”)* | | 🛑 **Frustration** | What’s broken, slow, or risky about how they’ve tried so far? *(e.g. “Tried 2 agencies, still no pipeline”)* | | 🧠 **Shortcut / Asset** | What do you give them that makes this easier or faster? *(e.g. “Done-for-you outbound engine: copy, list, follow-up, calls”)* | | 🔥 **Urgency Trigger** | Why now? What flips the switch? *(e.g. “Inbound dried up, forecast in danger”)* | --- ### Example: Building the Offer from Scratch **Audience:** CRM software founders **Persona:** Jake - stuck at $3M ARR, inbound plateaued, tried 2 failed agencies **Goal:** Book 15–25 quality demos/month without hiring an SDR **Frustration:** Still writing emails, managing inbox, leads don’t convert **Shortcut:** Proven system → full copy, list build, inbox management **Urgency Trigger:** Forecast is short, CEO breathing down neck, pipeline is dry --- ### **Final Offer Statement (Based on That Input):** > “We run the entire outbound operation for CRM founders at $2-5M ARR. We write the emails, build the lists, send the campaigns, and book the calls directly into your teams calendar. You just show up and close. We guarantee 25 qualified calls that show up, or we work for free.” > ✅ Specific to one ICP ✅ High-value outcome ✅ Proven shortcut ✅ Flips the risk ✅ Built straight from persona pressure --- ### ✍️ Now Write Out: **🎯 Target:** `_____________________________` **🥇 Desired Outcome:** `_____________________________` **🛑 Frustration:** `_____________________________` **🧠 Shortcut / Asset:** `_____________________________` **🔥 Urgency Trigger:** `_____________________________` ## 4: The Offer Formula Vault
ℹ️ → Turn your offer into messaging that makes your buyer say: “This was built for me.”
### Before You Write: Check Market Sophistication You’re likely selling into a **crowded market.** So ask yourself: - Has your promise been heard 100+ times before? - Does your offer sound like everyone else’s? - Are you selling *cold email* — or *the system that finally makes cold email work*? > “In a crowded market, your ‘what’ can sound familiar, but your ‘how’ must feel new.” > --- ### ❌ Don’t Say: > “We book meetings for B2B SaaS using cold email.” > ### ✅ Say: > “We install the outbound system that books 87 demos in 90 days and run it for you. We guarantee 25 qualified calls or we work free.” > --- ## Formula 1: Empathy-First **Format:** ### `[What They’re Struggling With] → [What They’ll Be Doing Differently] → [With What Shortcut or Proof] → [In How Long] → [Even If…]` This one leads with **pain and belief-breaking.** Best for **Problem- and Solution-Aware** buyers especially if they’ve been burned before. --- **Example (Lead Gen):** > Struggling to book quality calls that actually close? > > > We’ll install the same outbound system that booked 87 CRM demos in 90 days — and you’ll start seeing qualified calls land by the end of the week. > > Even if you’ve tried 3 agencies before. > --- **Example (Sales Training):** > Can’t close outbound leads? > > > You’ll use the same 8-question script we’ve use to close $30K+ deals from cold. > > No pitch decks. No 60-minute calls. Just real control. > > Even if you hate selling. > --- ## Formula 2: Trigger + Outcome **Format:** ### `[Trigger] + [Proof-Backed Asset] + [Fast, Clear Transformation]` This one hits **fast, proven, and clear.** Great for **Solution- and Product-Aware** buyers. --- **Example (Lead Gen):** > Inbound dried up? > > > Plug in the outbound system that booked 87 CRM demos and launch your first campaign in 48h, no reps required. > --- **Example (LinkedIn Growth):** > Losing reach on LinkedIn? > > > Use our content engine that turned 4 posts into $28K MRR and relaunch in 30 minutes flat. > --- ## Quick Awareness Fit Table | **Formula Type** | **Use When…** | **Buyer Awareness Level** | | --- | --- | --- | | Empathy-First | They’re stuck, skeptical, or burned | Problem- or Solution-Aware | | Trigger-Outcome | They’ve tried things, want results | Solution- or Product-Aware | --- ## ✅ Plug in Your Offer Now 1. Pick one format. 2. Fill in the blanks using your Offer Creation work from Section 3. 3. Write 3–5 versions. 4. Say each out loud. 5. Keep the one that feels like a **knife through butter.** --- ## Bonus: Hormozi’s Value Equation - In Plain English > Value = (Dream Outcome × Believability) ÷ (Time × Effort) > --- ### Ask Yourself: | **Question** | **Why It Matters** | | --- | --- | | 🔮 Dream Outcome | Is the result meaningful? → Bigger = more exciting | | ✅ Believability | Is it proven and de-risked? → Increases trust | | ⏳ Time | How fast do they win? → Faster = more desire | | 🔧 Effort | How hard is it for them? → Less effort = more yes | --- ### Example: Lead Gen Offer - “Book 87 demos in 90 days” → **Big win** - “We’ve done it before + guarantee it” → **Believable** - “Campaign live in 48h” → **Fast** - “No reps, no copy, no inbox to manage” → **Zero effort** ✅ When all 4 click, your offer sells itself. ## 5: Offer Stacking: How to Sell a System, Not Just a Service
ℹ️ → Stop selling just results. Start selling certainty.
### ❌ The Rookie Mistake > “We book you 15–25 qualified calls per month.” > Sounds fine on paper. But to a buyer? → “Cool. So do 500 other agencies. Why you?” You’re selling a **result**… …but not a **reason to believe.** --- ### ✅ What Makes an Offer Feel Premium? It’s not just **what** you do. It’s **how** you package it: - Do you de-risk it? - Do you give tools that save time? - Do you support them beyond just delivery? When buyers see your offer, they should say: > “I’m not just buying a service, I’m buying a whole system.” > --- ## The Offer Stack Template Start with your **base deliverable.** Then add bonus layers that increase clarity, speed, and confidence. | **Layer** | **What It Adds** | **Example** | | --- | --- | --- | | Base | The core deliverable | 15 qualified meetings/month | | Stack 1 | Tools or resources | Lead database with verified filters | | Stack 2 | Process accelerators | LinkedIn optimization checklist | | Stack 3 | Belief-builders | Sales call objection script | | Stack 4 | De-riskers | “15 show-ups or we work free” guarantee | --- Now your offer says: > “We don’t just book meetings. > > > We give you the list, the funnel, the messaging, the tools — and we eat the risk.” > ![Value Stack.png](Offers%20Build%20a%20No-Brainer%20Offer%20Your%20ICP%20Can%E2%80%99t%20Ign/Value_Stack.png) --- ## Positioning Your Stack Use phrases like: - “We do all of this so you don’t have to guess what works.” - “You get the same system that delivered 87 CRM demos in 90 days.” - “This isn’t just cold outreach. It’s your new outbound engine.” --- ### Your Goal: - → Turn a $1,500/mo service into a **$5K+ system** - → Make your buyer feel like they’re **skipping 6 months of trial and error** --- ## ✍️ Stack-Building Prompt Ask yourself: > “If I had to 5× the perceived value of this offer without adding more delivery work… > > > what **resources, belief-builders, or risk reversals** could I stack?” > Then list out **3–5 layers.** - What’s already done that you can include? - What tiny asset would make them feel safer or faster? - What bonus would a serious buyer say “yes” for *right now*? ## 6: Guarantees + Risk Reversal → The #1 way to make buyers say “fuck it, let’s try.” --- ⚠️ **QUICK NOTE:** If your buyer carries all the risk… They’ll delay, hesitate, or ghost you. --- ### ❌ What Most Founders Do - “We’ll do our best.” - “No guarantees.” - “It depends.” That’s not a pitch. That’s a gamble — and **your buyer’s the one holding the bag.** --- ### ✅ What High-Trust Offers Do They flip the risk. They signal confidence. They say: > “I believe in this so much, I’ll take the hit if it doesn’t work.” > --- ## 💬 How To Think About Guarantees A great guarantee increases: - **Believability** - “They must know this works.” - **Urgency** - “I’ve got nothing to lose.” - **Action** - “No more ‘thinking about it’ — let’s go.” --- ## 🎯 3 Types of Guarantees | **Type** | **What It Says** | **Example** | | --- | --- | --- | | 📆 Time-Based | "You'll get value fast" | “Get 5 qualified calls in 30 days or we work free” | | 🎯 Outcome-Based | "You'll hit your goal" | “15 show-up calls or you don’t pay” | | 🧠 Hybrid | "We’re flexible but firm" | “If we miss, you get a refund + bonus month” | --- ## Pick the One That Matches Your Offer - Selling **speed**? → Use **time-based** - Selling **revenue** or **appointments**? → Use **outcome-based** - Want to **filter for serious clients**? → Use **hybrid** --- ## 5 Risk-Free Risk Tips 1. **Qualify hard before you pitch** If they’re a bad fit, you’ll lose either way. Filter early. 2. **Cap the volume or timeline** Don’t say “unlimited” say “15 qualified calls in 30 days.” 3. **Make the buyer responsible too** “We need your feedback weekly, or the guarantee voids.” 4. **Use “Work For Free” over “Refund”** Feels more valuable, less scary. 5. **Use your stack to offset the cost** Even if they fail, they’ve still received assets, lists, playbooks. --- ## Real Example: Guarantee Rewritten **Weak:** “We’ll do our best to get you results.” **Strong:** “You’ll get 15 show-up calls that meet your exact criteria — or we work for free until you do.” --- ## ✍️ Write Yours Fill in this fast: > “You’ll get [result] in [timeframe] or [risk reversal].” > **Example:** “You’ll get 5 demos with decision-makers in 21 days — or we work for free until you do.” --- 📌 **REMINDER:** A guarantee doesn’t weaken your offer. It **proves** you believe in it more than they do. --- ✅ **Ready to sanity-check your offer?** Next we’ll test if your pitch actually passes the **7-second “I get it” filter**… or if it still sounds like work. ## 7: Offer Testing + Final Statement
ℹ️ → Is this actually a no-brainer… or just another nice idea?
## The 7-Second Offer Test > “If it takes more than 7 seconds to get your offer — it’s too vague.” > Ask yourself: | Question | ✅ / ❌ | | --- | --- | | 1. Does it say exactly who it’s for? | | | 2. Is there a clear, urgent result? | | | 3. Is there a shortcut or system? | | | 4. Is it specific + believable? | | | 5. Is the risk removed or flipped? | | ✅ If you’ve got **5 checkmarks,** you're good to go. ❌ If not, **tighten it.** --- ## Example: Bad vs. Great Offer **❌ Bad Offer:** > “We book B2B SaaS companies 15–20 meetings/month with decision-makers.” > - Too broad - No proof - Sounds like everyone else **✅ Great Offer:** > “We help CRM founders at $2–5M ARR build a scalable outbound engine using the same system that booked 87 demos in 90 days — and we guarantee 15 qualified, show-up calls or we work for free.” > - Speaks to the ICP - Shows proof - Flips the risk --- ## ✍️ Your Final Offer Statement Use this template: > “We help [ICP] get [result] without [friction] using [shortcut or proof] — and we guarantee [outcome or risk reversal].” > 👇 Fill in yours below: --- **Example:** > “We help CRM software founders at $2–5M ARR get 15+ qualified sales calls without hiring reps or managing inboxes — using the same outbound system that booked 87 demos in 90 days. We guarantee results or we work for free.” > Now you’re not selling **meetings**. You’re selling a **system** with a **guarantee** they believe. --- 🔁 **Final Reminder:** Your ICP + Persona told you **who** and **how** to sell to. Your **offer** is now the **bridge** between insight and conversion. Make it sound so good, they’d feel stupid saying no. ← Previous [Step 3: Offers + Positioning](/resources/step-3-offers-positioning-219f6d94771f80bea5f6ef818dbd4d67) Next → [Positioning: Sell More & Charge More](/resources/positioning-sell-more-charge-more-219f6d94771f80c48fa7d66bd87f028d) On this page

# Positioning: Sell More & Charge More # Positioning: Sell More & Charge More ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Positioning is the reason you close or you don’t You already have an offer. This page is about **making that offer feel premium**, urgent, and built for *them.* That’s how you charge more, get fewer objections, and close faster **without changing your service at all.** 🔁 Offers get ignored when positioning is weak. 💰 Great positioning makes buyers *want* to say yes before the pitch
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know exactly how to position your offer to feel 2–10× more valuable ✅ Sound like the **obvious** choice in a crowded market ✅ Understand why some agencies charge $1,500/mo and others $15K selling the same thing ✅ Have a 1-line position, system name, and “why us” story for your sales calls ✅ Use positioning to **raise your prices, pre-handle objections**, and win more inbound ## 1. Reframe: Why Positioning Helps You Sell More & Charge More --- Most founders obsess over **what** they sell. Smart founders focus on **how it’s seen.** That’s positioning. It’s not your logo. It’s not your colors. It’s not your LinkedIn headline.
**ℹ️ Positioning = the story your buyer tells themselves about what you're offering.**
--- ### Example: The Ferrari for $5K If I told you I had a Ferrari and I’d sell it to you for $5K, what’s your first reaction? > “What’s wrong with it?” > Same product. But the **price kills the perceived value.** Now flip it: If I show you 3 photos of happy owners, a clean title, engine stats, and a limited-time offer? Suddenly it’s a no-brainer. You’d **find the $5K.** ![ChatGPT Image Jun 20, 2025, 04_30_54 PM.png](Positioning%20Sell%20More%20&%20Charge%20More/ChatGPT_Image_Jun_20_2025_04_30_54_PM.png) --- ### Positioning Controls Perception | **Same Offer** | **Weak Positioning** | **Strong Positioning** | | --- | --- | --- | | Lead gen | “We book cold email calls for SaaS” | “We run your entire outbound engine and guarantee qualified meetings” | | Copywriting | “We write high-converting landing pages” | “We install conversion systems that doubled ARR for SaaS in the $2–10M range” | | Funnel Builds | “We design funnels” | “We rebuild your acquisition funnel to drive cash-on-day-one offers for high-ticket coaches” | Same core service. **Completely different perceived value.** --- ### 🎯 Goal of This Section: Make you realise positioning is **the difference between being ignored… and being irresistible.** ## 2. The 3 Positioning Plays ---
ℹ️  These are the 3 simplest, most effective ways to position your offer so buyers see it as premium before you pitch.
You don’t need clever copy. You need to choose **one clear lens** that makes your offer feel: ✅ Tailored ✅ Strategic ✅ Different from everything else That lens = your **Positioning Play.** --- ### Play #1: Category Reframe
ℹ️ Change what you're seen as.
--- Instead of being “another [service provider]” you create your own category. - You’re not a **cold email agency** - You’re an **Outbound Engine Partner** - You’re not a **copywriter** - You’re a **Conversion System** **Example:** > “Most founders hire cold email agencies. We build full outbound systems, copy, lists, nurturing, and booking. We run it end-to-end.” > **Mini Exercise:** > What new category makes your offer feel more strategic? > > > “We’re not a [what you do], we’re a [new category name].” > --- ### Play #2: Specialist Angle
ℹ️ Call it feel built for them.
--- The more narrow your focus, the easier it is to charge more. Generic = forgettable. Specific = valuable. **Example:** > “We do outbound for CRM SaaS founders at $2–5M ARR who’ve plateaued on inbound.” > Now it’s not just outbound, it’s a **diagnosis** with a **custom treatment.** **Mini Exercise:** > Who is this truly built for? > > > “We help [exact persona] who [struggle or goal].” > --- ### Play #3: System Advantage > Show your mechanism, not just your promise. > --- People don’t buy services. They buy **systems they trust.** If it looks repeatable, structured, and proven. You win the sale before it starts. **Example:** > “We install The 90-Day Pipeline Engine™ the same system that booked 87 demos in 90 days for CRM SaaS at $3M ARR.” > That’s not a service. That’s a **machine with a track record.** **Mini Exercise:** > What’s the name of your system? > > > “We install [system name] which delivers [outcome] in [timeframe].” > --- ### 🔁 Recap Table: Same Offer → Different Play → Different Price | **Core Service** | **Category Reframe** | **Specialist Angle** | **System Advantage** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Cold Email | “Outbound Partner” | “For $2–5M SaaS Founders” | “90-Day Demo Engine™” | | Funnels | “Acquisition Architecture” | “For Course Creators at $50K+/mo” | “Cash-on-Day-One Funnel™” | | LinkedIn Content | “Audience Monetization” | “For B2B Advisors > $10K offers” | “LinkedIn Growth Loop™” | --- ✅ Choose one play. ✅ Write the line. ✅ Use it in your emails, calls, bios, headlines. This is the fastest path to sounding more premium **without changing what you do.** ## 3. The 3 Pricing Levers ---
ℹ️ → Why positioning lets you charge more *without changing your service* You don’t need a better service. You need a better **frame.**
Most founders try to increase their price by “adding more.” More calls. More reports. More busy work. 🚫 That doesn’t work. ✅ What works: **changing how your offer is perceived.** --- ### Hormozi’s Value Equation (Positioning Edition) > Value = (Dream Outcome × Believability) ÷ (Time × Effort) > If you want to charge more, position your offer to: - **Promise a bigger win** - **Show it’s proven** - **Deliver it faster** - **Make it feel easy** That’s it. --- ### How Positioning Affects Pricing | **Lever** | **Positioning Strategy** | **Example** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Dream Outcome** | Be ultra-specific and ROI-linked | “Grow your LinkedIn” → ❌“Add $30K MRR with a content system” → ✅ | | **Believability** | Show proof + repeatable systems | “We’ll try our best” → ❌“Used by 87 CRM founders” → ✅ | | **Time** | Emphasise speed to first win | “It takes a few months” → ❌“First 3 calls in 72h” → ✅ | | **Effort** | Reduce how much they have to do | “You write copy” → ❌“We write, send, book and you just show up” → ✅ | --- ### 🔁 Quick Positioning Adjustments | **If you want to...** | **Do this** | | --- | --- | | Charge more | Position around ROI, not activity | | Get faster yeses | Show speed to win | | Remove price objections | Stack proof + risk reversal | | Reduce buyer effort | Emphasize your done-for-you process | ### ✍️ **Mini Exercise**: **1. Dream Outcome:** > “What’s the biggest, clearest win this creates?” > **2. Believability:** > “Where’s the proof this works?” > **3. Time:** > “What’s the fastest visible win I can promise?” > **4. Effort:** > “What work can I remove from the client?” > --- ✅ Lock these in → and pricing stops being a debate. You’re no longer justifying your price, you’re just explaining why it’s a no-brainer. --- ## 4. Value Stacking ---
ℹ️ Want to charge more without adding more? Break your offer down. Make the value visible.
You don’t need to invent more stuff. You just need to show buyers everything they’re *actually getting* in a way that feels: ✅ Tangible ✅ Premium ✅ Easy to say yes to That’s **Value Stacking.** --- ### Why Value Stacking Works Positioning isn’t just what you say. It’s **what they see.** When you **show the parts**, buyers understand the price. | ✅ What It Does | 🚫 What It Replaces | | --- | --- | | Turns your service into a system | “We just do cold email” | | Justifies higher prices | “Why does this cost $4K?” | | Makes intangible things feel real | “What am I actually paying for?” | --- ### Real Example: Turning a $300 Service into a $4K+ System Let’s say you charge $300 per qualified call. You want to switch to a monthly retainer model at $4,250/month. But you’re worried clients won’t “get” the price. Here’s how you **use value stacking to justify it:** | **Service Component** | **Description** | **Market Value** | | --- | --- | --- | | Email Deliverability Optimisation | Monitoring and optimisation of your domains to ensure deliverability + step-by-step guides for internal upkeep | $500/month | | Monthly Lead Generation | Targeted, list-built lead gen campaigns with campaign logic | $3,000/month | | Lead Nurturing | Sequences and logic to warm up long-cycle buyers over time | $1,500/month | | Inbox Management | We manage replies, chase no-shows, and book qualified meetings | $1,500/month | | Training Guides and SOPs | Assets for internal teams to scale and replicate results | $1,000/month | | Marketing Content Creation | Foundational cold email messaging, subject lines, angles, etc. | $500/month | | Unlimited Verified & Qualified Lead Lists | Always-fresh data sources curated for your ICP | $800/month | **🧾 Total Market Value: $8,800/month** You charge: **$4,250/month** --- > Now you’re not selling “calls.” > > > You’re selling a *system* with a **real market value** for nearly **50% less.** > That’s believable. That’s premium positioning. --- ### ✍️ Mini Exercise: Stack What You Already Do Build your own value stack here: | **Layer** | **What You Already Deliver** | **Market Value** | | --- | --- | --- | | Tool | `_______________________` | `$______` | | Asset | `_______________________` | `$______` | | Process | `_______________________` | `$______` | | Support | `_______________________` | `$______` | | Bonus | `_______________________` | `$______` | 💡 **Tip:** Pull directly from what you already do even if you’ve never “named” it before. --- ### 🎯 Goal of This Section: → Make your price feel **cheap for what they get** → Create a **systemic offer**, not a service menu → Build confidence that your $4K+ price isn’t ‘too much’ it’s ‘cheap’ --- You’ve now got: - A unique positioning lens - A system name - A full value stack --- ## 5. How Positioning Makes Sales Easier ---
ℹ️ This is why positioning makes sales easier before you even get on the call
--- ### 🔁 Reframe Most founders prep for sales calls by rehearsing scripts. But if your positioning is clear, specific, and premium. The **call gets easier before it starts.** Strong positioning does 3 things *before the pitch begins*: --- ### 1. **Shifts Perception** → You’re not just another agency Instead of: > “Can you help us with lead gen?” > It becomes: > “We saw your system and want to know how to work with you.” > Positioning makes your offer feel **designed,** not desperate. --- ### 2. **Filters the Buyer** → They show up pre-sold Instead of: > “We’re talking to a few other agencies…” > It becomes: > “We saw your case study and want that result too.” > When your positioning is built around **ICP fit + outcomes**, it pre-qualifies. --- ### 3. **Sets the Frame** → You lead, they follow Instead of: > “Tell me more about what you do.” > It becomes: > “So what does onboarding look like?” > You’re not “pitching your thing.” You’re walking them through a process. --- ## Example: Positioning That Pre-Sells Let’s say your positioning is: > “We install the 90-Day Pipeline Engine™ the same outbound system that books 87 demos for $2–5M CRM SaaS founders within 90 days guaranteed.” > Here’s what happens: - They assume you have a system - They believe it works (based on proof) - They want to more You're not trying to **build desire,** you’re managing **expectations and qualifying** --- ## ✍️ Mini Exercise: Check Your Positioning for Sales Fit Answer these: 1. **Does my positioning make it clear who this is for?** 2. **Is there a named system or method they can want?** 3. **Would someone read this and say: “How do we start?”** If not → tighten it using Section 2 + 3. --- ### 🎯 Goal of This Section: → Show you why positioning is your **first and strongest sales asset** → Help you avoid “freestyle” sales calls that force you to compensate for weak framing → Get you thinking: “If my positioning is solid, I won’t need to ‘sell’ I’ll just need to confirm fit” ## 6. Positioning Summary Formula ---
ℹ️ This is your **Positioning Formula.** No BS. Lock this in and you’ll instantly sound more premium across your entire GTM.
### ✍️ Fill This In Below Each line should be **tight, clear, and confidence-inducing.** Use your answers from Sections 2–4. --- ### 1. **Positioning Statement** What do you do, for who, with what result? > “We help [ICP] get [result] using [shortcut/system] — and we guarantee [X].” > **Example:** “We help CRM founders at $2–5M ARR get 15+ qualified sales calls without hiring reps — using our 90-Day Pipeline Engine™. We guarantee results or we work for free.” > Your Turn: > > > `We help ______________________ get ______________________ using ______________________. We guarantee ______________________.` > --- ### 2. **Category Name** What are you, really? (Not “cold email agency” — but something strategic) **Example:** Outbound Partner / Fractional Outbound Team > Your Turn: > > > `We’re not a [old label], we’re a ______________________.` > --- ### 3. **System Name** Name the machine behind the result. This is your mechanism. **Example:** The 90-Day Pipeline Engine™ / LinkedIn Growth Loop > Your Turn: > > > `Our system is called ______________________.` > --- ### 4. **Value Stack Bullets** What 3–5 things are they really getting? Pull from your value stack. > ✅ Email deliverability setup > > > ✅ Lead sourcing, copywriting, inbox mgmt > > ✅ Call booking + LinkedIn profile optimization > > ✅ SOPs + reporting > > ✅ All done-for-you > Your Turn: - `✅ ______________________` - `✅ ______________________` - `✅ ______________________` - `✅ ______________________` - `✅ ______________________` --- ### 5. **Price Justification Line** Make the price feel cheap for the value. > “Total market value: $8,800/month. You pay $4,250.” ”An SDR’s salary is $5.5K/month without commission, training, resources or recruitment costs” > > Your Turn: > > > `Total market value: $________. Our price: $________.` > --- ### 6. **“Why Us” Differentiators** 3 bullets that prove this was built for your buyer. > 🧠 Built specifically for $2–5M SaaS founders > > > 🧪 Proven system used by 87+ companies > > 🛡️ Fully managed with performance guarantee > > Your Turn: > - `🧠 ______________________` - `🧪 ______________________` - `🛡️ ______________________` --- ### ✅ When You’re Done You’ll have a one-pager you can drop into: - Sales calls - Cold email CTAs - Bios + landing pages - LinkedIn feature section This is your *Positioning Engine.* Make it feel like the obvious yes, because it is. ## 7. Final Positioning Test ---
ℹ️ You’re nearly done. Now to make sure this actually holds up in the real world.
A great position isn’t clever. It’s **obvious.** This final test forces you to sanity-check your positioning before you build outreach, landing pages, or sales calls around it. If you can’t say “yes” to every line - fix it. --- ### ✅ The 4-Point Clarity Test > Ask yourself: > > > If I dropped this in a cold DM or on a call, would someone instantly “get it”? > | **Test** | **Yes / No** | | --- | --- | | 1. Does a stranger know who this is for? | ✅ / ❌ | | 2. Does it feel different from competitors? | ✅ / ❌ | | 3. Does it justify a higher price? | ✅ / ❌ | | 4. Does it remove key objections or risk? | ✅ / ❌ | --- If you hit ✅ across the board → you’ve nailed it. If not → revisit Sections 2–4. You’re one tweak away. --- ### Enhancers Box: Optional Power-Ups Want to double the perceived value of your offer overnight? Stack these 3 “positioning enhancers” | **Enhancer** | **What It Does** | **Example** | | --- | --- | --- | | ✅ 1-Line Proof | Increases believability fast | “Used by 87 CRM founders across $2–10M ARR” | | 📸 Visual of the System | Makes it tangible + repeatable | Graphic showing 3-phase outbound engine | | ⏳ Scarcity Angle | Adds urgency + exclusivity | “We take 3 clients/month. Waitlist opens Friday.” | 💡 Use these in your: - Calendly descriptions - Email PS lines - Slide 1 of your deck - LinkedIn feature section --- ### ✅ **You’ve now locked in a positioning engine that:** - Makes your offer feel 10× more valuable - Filters the right buyers before the pitch - Justifies premium pricing without more work ← Previous [Offers: Buld a No-Brainder Offer](/resources/offers-build-a-no-brainer-offer-your-icp-can-t-ign-219f6d94771f80f29d6ff94d85fc2982) Next → [Step 4: Strategic Messaging](/resources/step-4-strategic-messaging-219f6d94771f80318b7fe975749309fa) On this page

**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Your GTM is dialled in. Angles sharp. Emails positioned as ‘buyer-first.’ But your emails could still get ignored… Why? **They don’t believe you.** This page shows you how to make every claim feel **impossible to doubt.**
## **The Trust Problem** --- You’re not ignored because your offer’s bad. You’re ignored because buyers lump you in with every other agency saying the same thing. - Buyers get **60–100 pitches/month**. - **15+ promise “performance-based, no risk.”** - They’ve been burned before. By the time they see *your* email, you’re pitch #19 that week. Without believability, you’re just the same as everyone else. --- ## **The 3 Drivers of Believability at Scale** --- There are the three levers that close the gap:
✅ **Messaging/Framing** → Make your claims sound beleivable. **✅ Social Proof** → Stack evidence they can’t argue with. ✅ **Assets** → Build stuff that screams “we’re legit” before you speak.
The rest of this page breaks each down. --- ## **1. Make Every Claim Believable** --- [**Make Every Claim Believable**](/resources/make-every-claim-believable-23bf6d94771f806c8851f8422274a69a) --- ## **2. # Build Proof Assets That Pre-Sell # **Build Proof Assets That Pre-Sell** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most of a buyer’s decision to trust you is made **before** they ever reply. Assets build that trust for you Assets warm the reply **and** stack trust for the call before you even show up.
## **1. What Assets Actually Do** --- Assets = **proof** - They make buyers think: *“These guys clearly know what they’re doing.”* - They answer doubts you can’t cram into email. One good asset = 10 clever follow-ups. --- ### **2. The Asset Creation Lens** --- Before you build anything, ask yourself:
1. **What would instantly make them believe I can fix *their* pain?** (*Not educate them. Prove I’ve solved it for someone like them.*) 2. **What’s the simplest way to *show* it instead of telling?** (*Screenshot, teardown, short Loom…*) 3. **Would this make them think “Finally, someone who gets my world”?** (*If not, don’t create it.*)
Your assets exist to **make replying feel safe**, not to look smart. --- ### **3. The Two Types of Assets** --- There are only two types that matter for campaigns: --- | **Type** | **Purpose (Campaign-Level)** | | --- | --- | | **Trust Assets** | Quick, skim-friendly proof that instantly shows you’ve solved *their* problem for people like them → grabs attention + earns first reply | | **Authority Assets** | Deeper proof that answers unspoken doubts (“Was it a fluke?” “Do they get companies like us consistently?”) → makes replying feel safe | ### **4. Examples That Work** --- ### ✅ **Trust Assets** --- Built to be scanned in 60 seconds: - **SEO** → 1-page teardown: *“Cut cost-per-lead 32% for [Similar Company]”* - **Paid ads** → 2-slide “campaign tweaks” deck: *“3 changes that doubled ROAS for [CompanyType].”* - **Lead gen** → Inbox screenshot or 60-sec Loom: *“18 booked calls in 30 days - here’s the trigger email.”* - **Branding/Design** → Before/after visual: *“New homepage doubled free trials.”* --- ### ✅ **Authority Assets (Belief Stackers)** --- Best used in later touches (follow up/s) to push warm or skeptical leads over the line.
- **“Wins in 30 Days” doc** → 10 Slack snippets or quick-win screenshots. - **Mini-case deck (3 slides)** → Before → What changed → After. - **Video interview with similar ICP** → *“We were stuck with X, Y, Z they fixed it in 30 days.”*
--- ### **5. When to Use Them (Campaign Logic)** --- **Trust first. Authority second. Hardest Authority last.**
✅ **Cold email #1** → 1 Trust Asset to break through noise. ✅ **Follow-ups** → Layer Authority Assets to answer doubts and build confidence. ✅ **Break-up email / Re-engage** → Hardest-hitting Authority (e.g., “Wins in 30 Days” doc or video case).
--- ### **6. Do This Now** --- 1. Ask: *“What’s the fastest thing I can SHOW that proves I’ve solved their pain?”* 2. Make **1 Trust Asset + 1 Authority Asset** this week. 3. Add the Trust Asset to your **next cold email #1** and send to prospects. --- ← Previous [Stack Proof That Sells For You](/resources/stack-proof-that-sells-for-you-23bf6d94771f808d8538c7c07cb40a73) Next → [Build Belief At Scale](/resources/build-belief-at-scale-23bf6d94771f80df8e28d8cddf3075dc) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Make Every Claim Believable # **Make Every Claim Believable** ---
How you frame your offer, your outcome, or your proof will have a huge impact on it’s beleivability. Most people will look to use the biggest and best claim they can. Perhaps because everyone doing $10K/mo selling courses tells you to “sell the outcome” Whilst that’s true. It’s not what the best do. The best know to sell emotion and believability and **THEN sell the outcome**. **Here are some examples 👇**
## **1. Why Framing Matters** --- Most founders default to big, vague promises.
- **Why?** It feels safe to write and every $10K/mo guru yells “sell the outcome!” - **The truth?** Buyers trust *specifics*. **Mechanisms, numbers, and risk feel real.** Everything else screams “spam.” **Your job:** sell believability first → outcome second.
--- ## **2. Before / After Examples** --- ### **Example 1** --- ❌ “We help SaaS companies get more booked calls.” --- ✅ “Booked 18 calls for [CompanyName], another Conversational Intelligence Platform, in just 30 days. Leading to 3 closed deals because all calls were with qualified buyers.” --- **Takeaway:**
Why it hits harder: 1. **Specific type** (“another Conversational Intelligence Platform”) → instantly signals *you get them.* 2. **Exact result + timeline** (“18 calls in 30 days”) → real numbers. 3. **Pre-handled their objection** (“qualified buyers”) → kills their #1 doubt upfront. Specific = More believable.
--- ### **Example 2** --- ❌ “We scale SEO agencies to $100K/mo.” --- ✅ “Helped 2 SEO agencies [CompanyName] & [CompanyName] - land 12 new clients each within 60 days, doubling their revenue.” --- **Takeaway:**
The difference is **stacked credibility**: 1. **Named proof** → instantly feels real 2. **Exact result** → “12 clients in 60 days” feels plausible. 3. **Outcome as consequence** → doubling revenue sounds logical
--- ### **Example 3** --- ❌ “We grow revenue fast.” --- ✅ “We’ll triple your website traffic in 14 days or you don’t pay.” --- **Takeaway:**
This works because it uses **risk + mechanism**: 1. **Mechanism first** → traffic is an input they can picture. 2. **Risk reversal** → “or you don’t pay” removes the biggest doubt. 3. **Time-bound** → urgency makes it feel real. You’re selling something measurable and safe to say yes to.
--- ## **3. The $30K Framing Rules** --- Boil every claim down to these 3 rules: ✅ **Specific over broad** → Numbers, timelines, named ICPs. ✅ **Mechanism first** → Inputs feel more believable than big outcomes. ✅ **Pre-handle objections** → Kill their #1 doubt inside the claim. --- ## **4. Do This Now** --- 1. Rewrite 2 of your strongest angles → add 1 number + 1 ICP reference. 2. Kill any line you can’t screenshot proof for. 3. Add 1 pre-handled objection to your highest-reply email.
**5 minutes spent reframing beats 50 new angles.** Believability makes angles convert.

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Stack Proof That Sells For You # **Stack Proof That Sells For You** ---
🔑 You’re losing deals because you’re not stacking proof like you should be. You don’t even recognise the proof you already have. Or worse, you think your results “aren’t good enough yet.” That’s **stupid.** Your prospect’s biggest fear is simple: **“What if they can’t get results?”**
--- ## **Why Proof Wins Deals** --- - Buyers don’t care about your “process.” - They care if you’ve **already delivered for people like them.** - One screenshot of a real win will close more deals than 1,000 “trust me” emails. - Proof short-circuits doubt. **Replies follow belief, not copywriting.**
You should be doing everything you can to collect as much proof as you can
## **Why You’re Not Stacking Proof (And Why It’s Dumb)** --- You’re probably guilty of all three:
❌ Not asking for testimonials because it feels awkward. ❌ Not pulling screenshots of quick wins. ❌ Telling yourself “I’ll wait until the results are bigger.” **Meanwhile:** Your competitors with worse results are out-selling you **because they show what they’ve got.**
--- ### **Imagine This…** --- You scroll Slack and your inbox for 20 minutes. You pull 10 screenshots of **quick wins from the first 30 days** with new clients. Then you drop them in a simple doc or on your site: **“Wins Generated for Clients in the First 30 Days.”** That single page of raw proof will close more deals than months of “personalised” outreach. --- ## **Start Collecting Video Testimonials** --- Yes, it feels awkward. Yes, you’ll feel like you “should wait.” But enough of that. Your next clients will buy because of your **track record.** You should be constantly asking for interviews. We’ve made this brain-dead simple: ### 👉 [**HOW TO COLLECT TESTIMONIALS (Exact Questions + Templates)**](/resources/collecting-testimonials-45e4ac0fa8154bfcba7725b413559014) --- ## **You Already Have Proof You’re Ignoring** --- If you’ve booked **3 calls** or closed **1 deal**, you have proof. Pull it now. Stop pretending you don’t. | **Hidden Asset** | **How to Surface It** | **Example** | | --- | --- | --- | | Calendar invites & meeting notes | Screenshot acceptance notifications | “Booked 14 demos in 11 days – buyer calendar screenshot.” | | Slack/Teams praise | Redact names & export snippet | “Client CMO: *‘Best pipeline month in 18 months.’*” | | Payment receipts | Blur card details | Stripe receipt showing **$12,800 ARR deal closed** |
One screenshot beats 1,000 words of “trust me.”
--- ## **Stack Proof Across Every Touch Point** --- Stack it. Don’t dump it. **Cold email #1** → One mini-case or stat. - **Follow-up #1** → Client quote or short Loom. - **Follow-up #2** → Different angle (cost cut, speed, ROI). - **Break-up email** → Stripe or invoice screenshot. **One proof. One ask. Every touch.** --- ## **6. Do This Now** --- 1. **Pull 10 wins** → Slack, email, or your inbox. 2. **Dump them in a folder** called “**Proof”.** 3. **Build a one-pager**: **“Wins Generated in the First 30 Days.”** 4. **Message every past and current client** asking for a quick video interview testimonial. ← Previous [Make Every Claim Believable](/resources/make-every-claim-believable-23bf6d94771f806c8851f8422274a69a) Next → [Build Proof Assets That Pre-Sell](/resources/build-proof-assets-that-pre-sell-23bf6d94771f8071b7e6cf08de62a92a) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Build Your Campaign Bible # **Build Your Campaign Bible** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE: A Campaign Bible is y**our **cheat code for cold campaigns**. It’s a running log of everything that works: angles, questions, decision makers, subject lines, objections etc. It turns random tests into a repeatable system. **It will be one of the single best things you ever do for your business**
## **Why It’s Powerful (For You)** --- Most founders guess every new campaign. Six weeks later, they’re back at zero. With a Bible: - ✅ **You know which angles hit and why** - ✅ **You test with intent** - ✅ **You see patterns faster → better replies, faster booked calls** - ✅ **You can hand it to a VA or SDR and they run it exactly how you do** --- ## **Why It’s Powerful (For Clients)** --- If you run lead gen, this is how you print money. Example: after just **2 SEO agency clients**, you’ve logged everything: - **The decision-makers who reply** - **The subject lines that get opens** - **The angles that work best** - **The CTA that gets “let’s talk”**
By client #3? You’re not “testing.” You’re running a **proven playbook**. **You can even offer this as a down-sell to non-qualified clients……**
--- ## **What It Looks Like** --- **Don’t overthink it.** It’s just a running document. ✅ **Tool?** Google Doc, Notion page, anything you’ll actually update. ✅ **Structure?** One page per ICP/offer. ✅ **Format?** Simple bullets + quick notes + results. ### **Example:** ---
**Offer:** Lead gen system for SEO agencies (booking qualified sales calls). **ICP:** SEO agencies doing $50K–$150K/mo, founder still closing deals. **Decision Maker:** Founder ### 🔥 Angles That Hit - **“How many deals are you missing out on?”** (example only) ### ✅ Best Subject Lines - “Quick question {{FirstName}}” (example only) ### ❌ Flopped **Subject lines:** - “More SEO leads” (example only) **Angles:** - “We book 15–20 calls/month for SEO agencies” ### 📝 Notes - Decision makers respond best when we implied **they’re leaving money on the table.** - Don’t mention “lead gen” or “cold email”
## **What Goes In It** --- Update weekly. Log everything. 1. **Buyer Snapshot** – Quick ICP/persona cheat sheet 2. **Angles + Messages** – Winners, losers, and notes on why 3. **Copy Variants** – Subject lines, openers, CTAs that pulled weight 4. **Proof** – Case studies, authority lines 5. **Objection Handling** – Best reply scripts 6. **Performance Notes** – Open, reply, and booked call rates --- ## **How to Use It** --- - **Build as you test** → Add notes live, not weeks later - **Start new campaigns here** → Proven angles first, experiments second - **Scale with it** → Hire a VA → hand them the Bible → instant replication - **Stack Bibles** → One per ICP/offer → over time, you own entire market playbooks --- ## ✅ **Do This Now** --- Open a doc. Name it: **“Campaign Bible – [Market/Offer]”** Add your first live campaign today. By client #3, you’ll know more about that market than most “experts” selling to it. ← Previous [Test, Diagnose & Scale Campaigns](/resources/test-diagnose-scale-campaigns-23bf6d94771f803181a5e34d4df80072) Next → [Build Belief At Scale](/resources/build-your-campaign-bible-23bf6d94771f80b39502ebc83740625d) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Build Your Lead Engine # **Build Your Lead Engine** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** I’ve spent the last 5 years building and refining what I now call the **Lead Engine Framework**. It’s the exact approach we use for: - **Our own outbound** - **Clients** - **Businesses I consult with 1:1** The reason it works is simple: it’s **forces** you to create a campaign system that does the job your campaigns should and it’s based on 1,000s of campaigns, across different markets, tested and refined until the patterns were clear.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know exactly which campaigns to run and why ✅ Understand how much sending capacity to use for each campaign ✅ See the campaign hierarchy developed across 1,000s of tests ✅ Have a clear plan to implement your own Lead Engine today
**🚨 Haven’t run campaigns yet?** Run **ONE** campaign for 30 days first. 1. **Start here:** Use the strongest angle from → [](../Turn%20Your%20GTM%20Into%20Killer%20Emails%20219f6d94771f80df9940d43541f68017.md)[**Turn Your GTM Into Killer Emails**](/resources/turn-your-gtm-into-killer-emails-219f6d94771f80df9940d43541f68017) 2. **Follow this:** Go through the → [](https://www.notion.so/Test-Diagnose-Scale-Campaigns-234f6d94771f804fa50ddf0ec5bf1329?pvs=21)[**Testing Section**](/resources/test-diagnose-scale-campaigns-23bf6d94771f803181a5e34d4df80072) 3. **Target this metric:** Hit **1.5%+ reply rate** before scaling. That campaign becomes your first **Base Campaign**. **Then come back to this page after 30 days and build the full stack.**
## **Step 1: Build Your Base Campaign (Your Engine)** ---
Your **Base Campaign** is the reliable, high-volume campaign that is always running and is what you rely on for consistent meetings It’s not about perfection - it’s about **volume first, predictability second**. Even if your “best” campaign only gets 1–2% reply rates, it becomes your engine because it will run non-stop.
[Build Your Base Campaign](/resources/build-your-base-campaign-23bf6d94771f80a2810be6b2750ebf63) --- ## **Step 2: Research & Development Campaigns (Your Tests)**
Once your engine runs, you need to **improve your numbers without risking your base volume**. R&D Campaigns are smaller, controlled tests designed to find your next great base campaign.
[**Research & Development Campaigns**](/resources/research-development-campaigns-23bf6d94771f80bcb005e5e5d560289a) --- ## **Step 3: Strategic Campaigns (Quick Wins)** ---
Strategic Campaigns target **high-intent, quick-win audiences** - like previously interested leads or companies showing buying signals. They won’t have enough volume to replace your base campaign, but they give you **fast wins every month** while feeding fresh demand.
[**Strategic Campaigns (Quick Wins)**](Build%20Your%20Lead%20Engine/Strategic%20Campaigns%20(Quick%20Wins)%2023bf6d94771f8086bd78c67c4ecf9998.md) --- ## **Step 4: Run the Lead Engine** ---
Once all 3 parts are live, you’ll be running a **predictable machine**: - **Base = steady booked calls** - **R&D = improving reply and booked-call rates over time** - **Strategic = quick wins and fresh demand layered on top** Now you combine this system with the numbers and calculators from *Know Your Numbers* to: ✅ **Scale with certainty** (know exactly how many inboxes and emails you need to hit any client goal)
## Next, we will show you how to: T**est, Scale & Diagnose your campaigns** ← Previous [Campaign Strategy](/resources/campaign-strategy-23bf6d94771f80c2a147cfe71300cc72) Next → [Test, Diagnose & Scale Campaigns](/resources/test-diagnose-scale-campaigns-23bf6d94771f803181a5e34d4df80072) On this page
