closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Campaign Strategy # **Campaign Strategy** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You know your numbers now. But math alone won’t get you to $30K/month. If you don’t have a **campaign strategy** to *use* those numbers, you’ll still: - Burn one campaign out → pipeline dries up. - Panic and rewrite copy randomly, hoping something hits. - Have no system to predict why reply rates or booked calls move. Most $5K–$10K founders stay stuck here. Not because they can’t write good emails — but because they have **no strategy to turn those numbers into a predictable pipeline**. This framework took me 5 years and hundreds of campaigns to refine. It’s the difference between feeling “busy” sending emails and **building a machine that feeds you booked calls every week.**
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Stop thinking “What campaign should I run?” and start thinking **“Each campaign should do a job.”** ✅ Use your numbers to actually **plan campaigns instead of guessing.** ✅ See the difference between how **$5K vs $30K operators** think about campaigns. ✅ Understand why **campaign strategy is the bridge between knowing your numbers and building a predictable pipeline.** ## **1. What Most People Do (The $5K Operator)** - **Run one generic campaign** to everyone (“all SaaS founders,” “any agency owner”). - **Tweak copy constantly**, hoping something hits — no system, just guesses. - **Never segment** or test angles properly (“too much work”). - **Don’t track or learn** → can’t tell why reply rates move. - **Panic when a campaign dies** → calls dry up overnight. - **Revenue stuck** → no predictable pipeline. This is why most founders spin their wheels: **they treat campaigns like random shots, not parts of a system.** --- ## **2. The Lead Engine Framework (The $30K Operator Strategy)** ---
Most $5K founders think in **“one campaign at a time.”** $30K operators think in **systems**. Your campaigns aren’t random blasts. Each campaign has a job: ✅ **One feeds you predictable calls every week.** **✅ One tests new angles and offers.** **✅ One hunts for bigger plays that unlock new demand.** This framework took me 5 years and hundreds of campaigns to refine.
### **The 3 Parts of the Lead Engine Framework** | **Campaign Type** | **What It Is** | **Why It Exists** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Base Campaign (Your Engine)** | Your reliable, high-volume workhorse. Start with your *best* performing campaign (even if “best” is still bad). Volume first, perfection later. | Predictable booked calls every week. It’s not the most exciting, but it’s the backbone that feeds revenue. | | **R&D Campaigns** | Smaller campaigns designed to test new angles, offers, or triggers. | Improve your numbers over time without risking your base volume. These are how you *find* your next great base campaign. | | **Strategic Campaigns** | Targeted plays with high-intent or quick-win audiences (e.g., previously interested leads). | Smaller volume, higher-conversion plays that give you easy wins and fresh demand while your base runs. | Think of it like this 👇
- **Base Campaign = engine** (steady calls) - **R&D = lab** (tests that improve your engine) - **Strategic = sniper shots** (quick wins you run every month)
![ChatGPT Image Jul 21, 2025, 01_53_01 PM.png](Campaign%20Strategy/ChatGPT_Image_Jul_21_2025_01_53_01_PM.png) ## **3. The Campaign Hierarchy (Overview)** ---
Now that you understand the 3 campaign types, here’s **how to prioritise which campaigns to run first** inside **R&D and Strategic.** Important: ✅ **Your Base Campaign is separate** - it’s just the best-performing campaign you can run consistently. ✅ **This hierarchy only applies to R&D + Strategic campaigns.**
| **Priority** | **Campaign Type** | **What It Is** | **Example** | **Reason** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **1 (Highest)** | **Strategic** | **Previously Interested Leads** | “You were interested three months ago, let’s revisit it.” | Easiest wins – they already know you. Run this every month. | | **2** | **R&D** | **Lookalike Campaigns** | “Apple bought this, and it improved employee engagement — we can do the same for you.” | Warmer than cold leads. Test first → if it works *and* has volume, promote it to a new base campaign. | | **3** | **Strategic** | **Intent-Based Campaigns** | Targeting hiring managers posting new jobs; pitching new-hire gifts. | Timely, relevant demand but usually smaller TAM. | | **4 (Lowest)** | **Base** | **Generic Cold Outreach** | “Hey, we do X, here’s how we can help. Interested?” | Lowest intent, but the only one with enough TAM to run non-stop as your engine. |
You’ll run Strategic and R&D campaigns **alongside** your base, not instead of it. Your base feeds predictable calls. These campaigns layer on top to give you quick wins and better data.
--- ## **4. Quick Example: Rachel** ---
Here’s exactly how we used this framework to turn Rachel’s underperforming campaigns into a system.
### **Step 1: Start With Her Base Campaign** Her best-performing cold campaign was only getting: - **Reply Rate:** 1.7% - **Positive Reply Rate:** 0.27% Not great, but enough to act as a **base campaign** because it had the **volume (large TAM)** to run non-stop. We didn’t scrap it. We reverse-engineered the math instead:
✅ With those ratios, she needed **X emails/day** to hit **Y booked calls/week** (using the same math from *Know Your Numbers*). ✅ We scaled inboxes to hit that daily volume first. This got her booking calls consistently - even with “bad” numbers.
--- ### **Step 2: Layer Strategic Campaigns for Quick Wins** While the base ran, we launched **2 Strategic Campaigns**:
1. **Previously Interested Leads** - Re-engaged contacts who showed interest 3–6 months ago. - Lower volume, but booked calls faster due to familiarity. 2. **Intent-Based Campaigns** - Scraped companies posting new job listings; pitched new-hire gift bundles. - Perfect timely angle, even with small TAM.
--- ### **Step 3: Use R&D Campaigns to Improve Her Base Numbers** We ran **Lookalike Campaigns** as R&D:
- Scraped similar companies to her best clients. - Tested new angles and hooks to see what bumped reply rates. When an R&D campaign started outperforming her base (e.g., 3%+ reply rate, 1%+ positive replies), we scaled it up - eventually replacing the old base campaign.
--- ### **The Result**
✅ Predictable weekly calls from the **base campaign**. ✅ Extra booked calls from **Strategic campaigns** every month. ✅ Gradually improved reply + positive rates through **R&D testing**, reducing how many emails she needed to hit her goals. ✅ Her best ever revenue month just 3 months after.
--- ## **5. Why This Works** --- - **Predictability First:** Your base campaign keeps calls coming regardless of testing results. - **Compounding Wins:** Strategic campaigns give you easy monthly wins. R&D campaigns slowly improve reply and booked-call rates, lowering your required volume over time. - **Systematic Learning:** Every campaign has a clear job → nothing is random. You know exactly what to kill, scale, or keep testing. - **Operator Mindset:** This is how $30K operators think — **engine first, experiments second, quick wins layered on top.** --- ## **6. Next Step → Build Your Lead Engine** --- Now you know **what** system you need to run. In the next section, we’ll build it: ✅ **Your Base Campaign (the engine that feeds predictable calls).** ✅ **R&D Campaigns (how to test angles without killing your volume).** ✅ **Strategic Campaigns (the big plays that unlock new demand).** ← Previous [Know Your Numbers](/resources/know-your-numbers-23bf6d94771f8027883ddbeb0b217f8e) Next → [Build Your Lead Engine](/resources/build-your-lead-engine-23bf6d94771f809fa92ddf3a01596a9e) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Know Your Numbers # Know Your Numbers ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** If you don’t know your numbers, you’re driving blind. Most founders “feel” like a campaign is working or not — but feelings don’t scale. **This page makes your campaigns predictable.** 👉 **We are NOT fixing campaigns here.** 👉 **We are only tracking, calculating, and knowing the volume you need to hit your goal.** Diagnosis and improvement come later. For now, you just need clean, reliable numbers.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know exactly which numbers to track (and which ones to ignore) ✅ See if your results are good or bad by comparing to proven benchmarks ✅ Work out how many emails you need to send to hit any goal ✅ Figure out how many emails you can send each week and if you’re using your inboxes to full capacity ✅ Have a clear baseline so you know what to expect before changing anything ## **1. What to Track (The Core Metrics)** --- What metrics to track and the benchmarks you should aim for | **Metric** | **Target** | | --- | --- | | **Reply rate** | **Aim for 2.5%** | | **Overall positive Reply rate** | **1% (Minimum)** | | **Positive reply as % of replies** | **40%** | | **Meeting booked rate** | **0.2% - 0.5% (industry average 0.05 - 0.1%)** | | **Positive Reply to Meeting Conversion Rate** | **25% (Minimum)** | | **Call show-up rate** | **80%** | | **Bounce rate** | **<2%** | | **Leads To Booked Call Ratio** | **Aim for ~500; (under 300 is great)** | | **Close Rate** | **Aim for ~30%** | ### **Why These Metrics Matter** --- | **Metric** | **Why It Matters** | | --- | --- | | **Reply Rate** | Shows if you’re even reaching the right people (targeting & list quality issue if low). | | **Positive Reply %** | Tells you if your message resonates (offer or copy problem if low). | | **Meeting Booked Rate** | Shows if replies are turning into booked calls | | **Positive Reply → Meeting Conversion** | Proof of believability in offer and CTA | | **Show-up Rate** | Tells you they believe you can solve their problem + they’re interested | | **Bounce Rate** | Not knowing it can destroy deliverability | | **Leads → Booked Call Ratio** | The single most important number that lets you scale with math instead of hope. | | **Close Rate** | You know how many calls you’re going to close |
When you know these numbers, you’ll know: - Exactly where your bottleneck is (targeting, copy, or sales). - How many emails you need to send to hit your booked call goal. - What to fix first to improve efficiency. **Outreach becomes math You have a formula Not some abstract riddle you don’t know how to solve**
## **2. How to Track** ---
Tracking isn’t complicated - 90% of what you need is already inside your email tool (Instantly, Smartlead, etc.) The rest you calculate manually using simple formulas.
### **Where to Find Each Metric** ✅ Reply Rate, Positive Reply %, Bounce Rate → Found directly in your ESP analytics. ✅ Meeting Booked Rate, Positive Reply → Meeting Conversion, Show-up Rate → Track manually in a simple spreadsheet or your CRM. ✅ Leads → Booked Call Ratio → Calculate it yourself (formula below). ### **Simple Formulas** | **Metric** | **Formula** | | --- | --- | | **Meeting Booked Rate** | (Meetings Booked ÷ Emails Sent) × 100 | | **Positive Reply → Meeting Conversion** | (Meetings Booked ÷ Positive Replies) × 100 | | **Show-up Rate** | (Calls Showed ÷ Calls Booked) × 100 | | **Leads → Booked Call Ratio** | Total Leads Contacted ÷ Total Calls Booked | | **Close Rate** | (Clients Closed ÷ Calls Showed) × 100 | ## **3. Turn Outreach Into Math (The Formula)** ---
Once you know your numbers, hitting any goal becomes simple math. **Here’s how to work it out:**
### **Example: Goal = Close 2 Clients/Month** Let’s plug in realistic numbers: | **Metric** | **Your Number** | | --- | --- | | **Leads → Booked Call Ratio** | 500 leads per booked call | | **Show-up Rate** | 80% | | **Close Rate** | 25% | --- ### **Step 1: Work Backwards From Your Goal** You want **2 clients per month**. **Close Rate = 25%** → You need **8 showed calls** to close 2 clients (2 ÷ 0.25). **Show-up Rate = 80%** → You need **10 booked calls** to get 8 showed calls (8 ÷ 0.8). **Leads → Call Ratio = 500** → You need **5,000 leads contacted** to book 10 calls (10 × 500). --- ### **Step 2: Scale With Certainty** - Want **4 clients a month?** → **20 booked calls** → **10,000 leads**. The better you make your numbers (e.g., reduce leads → call ratio from 500 to 300), the fewer emails you need to hit the same goal. --- ### **Step 3: Focus on One Bottleneck at a Time** - High leads → call ratio? → Fix targeting & copy. - Low show-up rate? → Fix reminders. - Low close rate? → Fix your sales process. ---
**The point:** You’ll never guess how many emails to send again. Outreach = math. Goals become achievable and predictable
## **4. Understand Your Sending Capacity** ---
Knowing your numbers is pointless if you don’t know how many emails you can actually send. Your sending capacity = how many emails you can send each day/week based on: ✅ **How many inboxes you have** ✅ **How many emails you send from each inbox per day** ✅ **How many emails are in your sequence (follow-ups eat into capacity)** ✅ **How many days per week you’re mailing** If you don’t know this, you might: – Think you’re “sending enough” when you’re nowhere near target – Burn time testing copy when the problem is just *volume*
### **Why Sending Capacity Matters** | **Reason** | **What It Means** | | --- | --- | | **You can’t scale what you can’t count** | You need to know if you have enough daily volume to hit your booked call goal. | | **Follow-ups reduce new leads** | The more follow-ups in a sequence, the fewer new leads you hit each day. | | **Inboxes = growth throttle** | The only way to scale further (without better ratios) is more inboxes or higher daily sends. | --- ### **Manual Math (If You Want to Work It Out)** - **Total Daily Capacity =** Inboxes × Emails Per Inbox - **New Leads Per Day =** (Total Daily Capacity ÷ Emails in Sequence) - **New Leads Per Week =** New Leads Per Day × Mailing Days --- ### **The Easy Way** You can work this out manually but we created **the Outbound Math Calculator to do it for you in seconds**. The calculator will also let you flip it: tell it how many clients you want per month, and it’ll tell you exactly how many inboxes and daily emails you need.
**⚠️ IMPORTANT: You should be monitoring how many emails you’ve actually been sending each day within your email tool to make sure it’s hitting capacity**
## **5. The Outbound Math Calculator** ---
The Outbound Math Calculator turns everything you’ve learned so far into a simple, fast calculation. No more spreadsheets. No more mental math. Just input your numbers and know exactly: ✅ **How many new leads you can hit daily & weekly with your current inboxes** ✅ **If your current capacity is enough to hit your booked call and client goals** ✅ **How many inboxes & emails you’ll need to hit any revenue target**
### **How It Works** The calculator has two modes: | **Mode** | **What It Does** | | --- | --- | | **Sending Capacity** | Work out how many new leads you can hit each day and week based on your inboxes, sequence length, and mailing days. | | **Leads → Clients Math** | Reverse-engineer from your client goal → tells you how many leads, emails, and inboxes you need to hit it. | --- ### **What You’ll Need** You already have these numbers from the previous sections: ✅ **Leads → Booked Call Ratio** (from your campaign metrics) ✅ **Close Rate & Show-up Rate** (from your call tracking) ✅ **How many inboxes you currently have** ✅ **How many emails you plan to send per inbox per day** ✅ **Total emails in your sequence (incl. follow-ups)** ✅ **Mailing days per week** Plug them in, and the calculator does the rest. --- ## **Use the Calculator Now** # 👉 [**Open the Outbound Math Calculator**](https://www.closingclientsgroup.com/outbound-math-calculator) ![Screenshot 2025-07-22 at 09.09.16.png](Know%20Your%20Numbers/Screenshot_2025-07-22_at_09.09.16.png) ← Previous [Build The Campaign Engine](/resources/build-the-campaign-engine-that-books-calls-233f6d94771f80128b07c3ad30beedb3) Next → [Campaign Strategy](/resources/campaign-strategy-23bf6d94771f80c2a147cfe71300cc72) On this page

# Test, Diagnose & Scale Campaigns # **Test, Diagnose & Scale Campaigns** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Every winning campaign starts as an idea You’ve built your engine (Base), you’ve run quick wins (Strategic). Now you need to **Test → Diagnose → Scale** Turning good campaigns into great ones that can predictably generate calls for your agency without you constantly having to worry.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this section, you’ll: ✅ Run clean, controlled tests that prove what works ✅ Pinpoint exactly why campaigns succeed or fail ✅ Scale your winners safely so they keep performing at volume ## **Step 1: Testing Campaigns** --- [**Testing Campaigns**](/resources/testing-campaigns-23bf6d94771f805c9811db214ef8ba07) --- ## **Step 2: Diagnose and Fix Campaigns Fast** --- [**Diagnose and Fix Campaigns Fast**](/resources/diagnose-and-fix-campaigns-fast-23bf6d94771f80cd8429db9891d2687b) ## **Step 3: Scaling Winners** --- [**Scaling Winners**](/resources/scaling-winners-23bf6d94771f80368e09c69494a20dd4) ← Previous [Build Your Lead Engine](/resources/build-your-lead-engine-23bf6d94771f809fa92ddf3a01596a9e) Next → [Build Your Campaign Bible](/resources/build-your-campaign-bible-23bf6d94771f80b39502ebc83740625d) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.title: Build Your Base Campaign
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Build Your Base Campaign # Build Your Base Campaign ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Your **Base Campaign** exists for **ONE reason**: ✅ **To book calls.** If it’s not: - Running **every single day** - **Using 80% of your daily sending capacity** - Hitting the daily volume you calculated in [Know Your Numbers](/resources/know-your-numbers-23bf6d94771f8027883ddbeb0b217f8e) …then it isn’t what I’m telling you to do. This is MY framework. I built this because it WORKS. This is the engine that pays the bills. It doesn’t need to be “amazing” It just needs to run.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Pick your base campaign **today** ✅ Know exactly **how much daily volume to run** to hit your booked-call goal. ✅ Understand the **rules you can’t break** if you want predictable results. ✅ Be able to **track if it’s running correctly** in your sending tool. --- ## **1. How to Find Your Base Campaign** --- ### **STEP 1: Pick Your “Best Available” Campaign**
Your Base Campaign is **NOT** your highest-reply campaign. It’s the one you can run **every single day, for months, without running out of leads.**
--- ✅ **RULES (Fast Version)** **✅ TAM big enough→ Must run daily for 4+ months minimum** ✅ **Best-performing generic campaign** → Broad, not hyper-segmented ✅ **Must have booked calls before** → Even if rates aren’t “the best” ✅ **Scalable** → Broad > segmented. Volume > perfection --- ### **The Detailed Version** ### **1. Must Have Enough TAM (Total Addressable Market)** --- Your base campaign **only qualifies** if it can run for **months** at daily sending volume. - ❌ **Too small:** Hyper-segmented (e.g., 200 loyalty-platform SaaS founders) - ❌ **Too small:** Lookalike with only 500 similar companies - ✅ **Good:** Broad, best-performing generic campaign (e.g., 40K+ SaaS companies 200–500 staff, targeting owners or marketing heads) If it can’t feed your daily sending capacity every week, **it’s not a base campaign — it’s an R&D or Strategic play.** --- ### **2. Must Have Proven It Can Book Calls**
Even if the reply/positive rate isn’t “great,” if it’s the best you’ve run so far and has booked calls → ✅ **it qualifies**.
### **3. Must Be Scalable**
If it can’t sustain 4**+ months of daily sending**, it can’t be your base. 👉 **Broad > segmented. Volume > perfection.**
### **STEP 2: Volume First, Perfection Later** ---
Your Base Campaign exists for **one reason**: 👉 **To book calls every week, not to win awards for reply rates.** Pick the **broadest, highest-volume campaign** you can run for months
--- ✅ **Examples** - **Good Base Campaign** → 1.7% reply rate, 0.27% positive, TAM = 10K SaaS companies ✅ - **Bad Base Campaign** → 5% reply rate, TAM = 150 companies ❌ (too small = R&D or Strategic play) ---
💡 **Think of your base as your parachute:** You don’t cut holes in it mid-air because you want a better one. You keep it intact until you’ve tested and certified a new one.
--- ### **STEP 3: Calculate Your Daily Volume** ---
Your Base Campaign is pointless if it’s not hitting the **daily volume required to reach your call goals.**
--- ✅ **Use the math from *Know Your Numbers*** - **Emails/day needed:** Based on reply + positive rates → how many emails you need to hit your booked-call target. - **Inboxes required:** How many sending accounts you need to hit that daily volume. - **Capacity split:** 80% of your total sending volume goes to your **base**, 20% reserved for **R&D/Strategic**. --- ## **2. Base Campaign Rules (Do Not Break These)** ---
### **Base Campaign Checklist** ✔ Always running (no gaps, no pauses). ✔ 80%+ of daily sending capacity goes to this campaign. ✔ No testing allowed → exact same copy, same segment that worked before. ✔ TAM big enough → must be able to run for at least 4+ months without running out of leads. ✔ Monitor daily volume → check your ESP analytics every morning.
![ChatGPT Image Jul 22, 2025, 02_02_55 PM.png](Build%20Your%20Base%20Campaign/ChatGPT_Image_Jul_22_2025_02_02_55_PM.png) --- ## **3. Monitor It (Daily)** ---
Monitor it **every single day**.
✅ Check your base campaign’s **sending analytics tab daily**: - Is it hitting your required daily volume? - If a day drops under your target volume, fix it immediately. This is not “set and forget”. --- ## **4. Real Base Campaign Example** ---
Rachel’s best-performing campaign was:
- **1.7% reply rate** - **0.27% positive replies** But it targeted a **broad TAM** (companies 20–200 employees, owners). So we used it as her Base Campaign.
✅ **Step 1: Scaled inboxes** → sent enough daily volume to hit her booked-call goal. ✅ **Step 2: Never touched the copy/segment** - it stayed exactly the same. ✅ **Step 3: Checked ESP analytics daily** → fixed any drops immediately.
Even with “bad” numbers, she booked steady calls. R&D campaigns later improved the reply rate, but **this base campaign funded her growth from Day 1.** --- ## **5. Summary (Your Next Steps)** --- ✅ **Pick your base campaign today** ✅ **Make sure it has TAM to run** ✅ **Run it every day, 80%+ of your sending capacity.** ✅ **Do NOT change anything** until you’ve found a better campaign than can consistently outperform it through R&D testing ✅ **Check daily volume** every morning. ← Previous [Build Your Lead Engine](/resources/build-your-lead-engine-23bf6d94771f809fa92ddf3a01596a9e) Next → [Research & Development Campaigns](/resources/research-development-campaigns-23bf6d94771f80bcb005e5e5d560289a) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.title: Research & Development Campaigns
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Research & Development Campaigns # **Research & Development Campaigns** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Your Research & Development campaigns are **NOT your engine**. They’re your **tests.** You don’t bet the business on them. You don’t feed them 80% of your sending capacity. Their only job: **beat your Base numbers without blowing up your pipeline.** This is where you test angles, hooks, and approaches to find the next Base campaign. 10% capacity (can be increased slightly if needed) Think of it like this: ✅ **Base = keeps the lights on** ✅ **R&D = builds a better campaign for tomorrow**
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end, you’ll: ✅ Understand what R&D campaigns are (and aren’t) inside your Lead Engine ✅ Know how much capacity to give them and why it stays small ✅ Learn what qualifies as an R&D test (and when it graduates to Base) ## **1. What Research & Development Campaigns Are** ---
R&D campaigns are **controlled tests** you run alongside your Base to try and beat and replace your base campaign over time. This is so we can reduce the volume needed to hit your cal targets (Doens’t matter if your reply rate is 80% - Always try and improve. Message and offer fatigue is real. Keep ahead of the market).
### **They are:** ✅ Tests designed to beat your Base campaign ✅ Capped at ~10% of sending capacity until proven ### **They are NOT:** ❌ A second Base campaign ❌ Something you rely on for predictable meetings --- ## **2. What to Test in R&D** --- You’re testing **one variable at a time** to see if you can beat your Base campaigns numbers: ✅ **Angles** – Different pain or outcome focus ✅ **Hooks** – New lead-in or unique insight ✅ **Offers** – Different guarantee or CTA ✅ **Targeting tweaks** – Same TAM, different titles or sub-segments ---
You’ll learn **exactly how to design and run these tests** **in**: [**Test, Diagnose & Scale Campaigns**](https://www.notion.so/Test-Diagnose-Scale-Campaigns-234f6d94771f804fa50ddf0ec5bf1329?pvs=21)
--- ## **3. When to Replace Your Base Campaign** --- ### **PROMOTE an R&D campaign to Base ONLY when:**
✅ It beats your Base campaign’s **reply → positive reply ratio** consistently ✅ It has held those numbers for **5K+ leads (not emails) or 30+ days** ✅ It has **4+ months of TAM** (enough volume to run daily) Everything else = still a test.
## **4. Rules You Can’t Break** --- ### **R&D Campaign Checklist:**
- [ ] ~10% of daily sending capacity (Base keeps 80%+) - [ ] One variable per test → no guessing games - [ ] Kill fast → under 1% reply after ~1K sends - [ ] Run consistent tests
--- ← Previous [Build Your Base Campaign](/resources/build-your-base-campaign-23bf6d94771f80a2810be6b2750ebf63) Next → [Strategic Campaigns](Strategic%20Campaigns%20(Quick%20Wins)%2023bf6d94771f8086bd78c67c4ecf9998.md) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.title: Strategic Campaigns (Quick Wins)
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Strategic Campaigns (Quick Wins) # **Strategic Campaigns (Quick Wins)** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Strategic Campaigns are **NOT your engine.** They’re the **quick-wins** you run **on top of your Base** to book easy calls every month. You’ll never replace your Base with these, the TAM is too small. But: ✅ They convert faster (warm leads + intent signals = higher reply rates) ✅ They’re repeatable → you can run them every month ✅ They’re perfect for quick client wins if you run lead gen
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end, you’ll: ✅ Know exactly what Strategic Campaigns are (and aren’t) ✅ Understand which campaigns to run first (and why) ✅ Be able to run your first Strategic campaign **today** even if you have no CRM --- ## **1. What Strategic Campaigns Are** ---
Strategic Campaigns are **high-intent, small-TAM campaigns** that add **extra booked calls on top of your Base.**
You run them: ✅ **Every month** ✅ **Alongside your Base and R&D campaigns**
⚠️ Stick to ~**10% of your capacity** If you can’t afford more inboxes to be able to run all types of strategic campaigns, choose **ONE at a time in order of priority** (see hierarchy below).
--- ### **They ARE:** ✅ Quick-win campaigns you can repeat monthly ✅ Perfect for warm leads, similar companies, or intent signals ### **They are NOT:** ❌ Your daily engine ❌ Something to rely on for predictable meetings --- ## **2. The Strategic Campaign Hierarchy** ---
You have **3 types of Strategic Campaigns.** Run **all 3 every month** if you have enough inboxes. If capacity is limited, **follow this order:**
--- ### **#1. Previously Interested (Run First, Every Month)** ---
**Why:** Easiest wins. These people already know you. Were already interested **Who to target:** --- - Anyone who replied positively but ghosted - People who booked calls but didn’t show - Anyone who attended a call but didn’t close - Old leads who showed interest 3–6 months ago --- **How to find them:** --- - **No CRM?** → Scroll your inbox or download Instantly/Smartlead reply lists - **CRM?** → Filter past opps, automate re-engagement --- **Angle:** --- > *"Hey, we spoke X months ago re [pain] and [solution] if you haven’t solved this yet…"* > **Volume:** --- Refill every month as new warm leads stack up.
### **#2. Lookalike Campaigns (Run Monthly or Quarterly)**
**Why:** Companies similar to your best clients convert faster than cold. **Who to target:** --- - Same industry, size, and roles as your best customers --- **How to find them:** --- - PandaMatch - Clay - Apollo Check out our → [**Lookalike Scraping Blueprint**](https://resources.closingclientsgroup.com/outreach/list-building/step-by-step-for-each-platform#block-7536ff54d528450389d619bc81a41238) --- **Angle:** --- > *"We helped [similar company] do X. Want the same result?"* > **Volume:** --- Fresh batch every quarter if TAM allows
### **#3. Intent-Based Campaigns (Pick the Best Triggers for YOUR Buyers)**
**Why:** Timely signals = higher conversion rates. **Your job:** --- Act like a founder. **Choose the 1–2 buyer signals that make the most sense for your market**. Don’t just copy anyone else; think about what shows the highest intent for **your** prospects. --- **Common triggers by market** (use for inspiration. Pick the ones that fit your best customers): --- - **Leadgen:** • **Hiring for SDRs** / Account Managers / Sales Directors (“Saw you’re hiring SDRs—here’s how we help sales teams hit quotas faster.”) • **CRM or tech stack change** (“Congrats on implementing HubSpot - here’s how we help you get ROI faster.”) - **SaaS →** • **Recent funding round** (“Congrats on your Series A - here’s how we help funded teams scale via outbound.”) • **New product launch or major feature** (“Loved your new feature - here’s how we drive adoption for similar releases.”) - **E-comm & Retail →** • Big marketing spend detected (Ad Library insights) • New store opening or product launch (press/announcement alerts) --- **How to run it:** --- - Pick your top 1–2 signals. - Build a list every month - Send, track replies → booked calls → conversion. --- **Volume:** --- Build fresh lists each month. If you have extra capacity, run both signals in parallel, if not, start with the highest-priority trigger and move down your list next month.
## **3. When and How to Run Them** ---
**Plan your campaign of quick wins alongside your Base (80%+) and R&D (10%).**
**If you’ve got extra inboxes:** --- - Run **all three** Strategic campaigns every month (Previously Interested + Lookalike + Intent-Based) --- **If you’re capped at 10% capacity:** --- - Run **one** Strategic campaign at a time, in this order: 1. Previously Interested 2. Lookalike 3. Intent-Based --- **Expect:** --- - **5–10 booked calls** per campaign per month. - **Stack** these small wins on top of your Base → extra revenue without risk. ## **4. Rules You Can’t Break** --- - [ ] Base stays 80%+ of sending capacity (never cut it for Strategic) - [ ] One Strategic campaign live at a time if you’re capped at 10% - [ ] Follow the hierarchy — don’t waste time on intent signals before warm lists - [ ] Stop when TAM dries up → refill next month --- ## **5. Summary & Next Steps** --- ✅ **This month:** Run a **Previously Interested campaign** (warmest wins first) ✅ **Next month:** Add Lookalike, then Intent-Based when capacity allows ✅ Keep Base running daily - Strategic is the **layer, not the engine** ← Previous [Research & Development Campaigns](/resources/research-development-campaigns-23bf6d94771f80bcb005e5e5d560289a) Next → [Build Your Lead Engine](/resources/build-your-lead-engine-23bf6d94771f809fa92ddf3a01596a9e) On this page

# Diagnose and Fix Campaigns Fast # **Diagnose and Fix Campaigns Fast** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This is where you come when something’s not working. You won’t to have to guess anymore when something is going wrong with your campaings. **One symptom → one fix → stop.**
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Spot what’s actually broken (infra, offer, copy, list). ✅ Pick the right fix in under 60 seconds ✅ Stop burning domains & time ✅ Recover reply & booked call rates without nuking everything ## ❌ **What Most People Get Wrong** --- - **They panic and change everything** → new angles, new domains, new lists all at once = no clear data. - **They blame the wrong thing** → poor offer blamed on “deliverability,” or list blamed on copy. - **They keep sending while diagnosing** → burning domains while “testing.” - **They track the wrong signals** → opens, click‑rates, or gut feel instead of replies.
This map fixes all that.
## **Cold Email Campaign Triage Map** --- ### **1. 10‑Second Table (SYMPTOM → FIX)** | **Symptom** | **Probable Root Cause** | **How To Fix It** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Bounce rate over 2%** | Bad list hygiene. You’re sending to invalid emails. | **1)** Pause all sending **2)** Re‑clean the list with MillionVerifier **3)** Relaunch campaign | | **Reply rate under 1%** | Deliverability issues, emails aren’t reaching inboxes | **1)** If using cheap infra such as Cheapinboxes etc you may need to replace those inboxes **2)** If using legacy infra Outlook/Google inboxes → run inbox tests + follow the Deliverability Fix section [Deliverability](/resources/deliverability-d9032c1a093b4bd18b7be85e24f1f109) | | **Reply rate 1–3% AND positive replies under 15%** | Weak offer or wrong angle for this ICP | **1)** Keep the same sending setup **2)** Write 3 very different angles (change one thing at a time) → **3)** Send 500-1K leads per angle, keep the best performer and kill the others | | **Positive replies are fine but booked calls under 30%** | Slow or weak follow‑up – you’re losing good leads in the inbox | **1)** Reply to leads within 5 minutes **2)** Make sure you’re offering a time to speak and NOT providing a call booking link | | **Reply rate drops 50% halfway through a campaign** | Copy has likely been fingerprinted or flagged by spam filters | **1)** Test your inboxes with the exact same copy – if landing in spam, scrap the email completely **2)** Rewrite fresh and make sure to use plain‑text only (don’t track opens) **3)** Resume on same sending setup to confirm it was copy‑related (after having removed blacklisted inboxes) | | **Reply rate stuck at 2–3% after multiple new angles** | Likely a targeting x messaging issue | **1)** Go back through our GTM, Buyer Persona and Find Your Angles sections **2)** Use our email writer to create new emails **3)** Run new campaign with new copy | | **New domains get blocked on day 1** | No warm‑up – sending too many emails too fast on fresh domains | **1)** Follow the guidance from your inbox provider or Closing Clients’ recommended sending volume per inbox [Campaign Set Up](/resources/campaign-set-up-dde64e0a0815402ea10aec9473f4a15e) | ### **2. Edge‑Cases (Quick Answers)** --- | **Signal** | **What It Means** | **Fix** | | --- | --- | --- | | **High replies > 5% but 80% negative** | Click‑bait hook misaligned with offer | Re‑align promise → tighten subject line | | **“You’re sending too many, try again later”** | Rate‑limit | Cut volume 50% → stagger send‑times → re‑warm slowly | | **Sudden jump in “unsubscribe” replies** | List fatigue | Swap list source → refresh with <30‑day-old data | --- ## Summary --- If you’re following the full Closing Clients system ✔ Proper **Inbox Setup** & **Campaign Setup** ✔ Writing emails using **GTM**, **Find Your Angles**, and the **Email Generator Tool** …you shouldn’t need this page often. But when something *does* break: **Come back to this page** Don’t overthink, don’t change everything at once, and don’t panic-send while “testing.” Come here, follow the row that matches your signal, get unstuck fast, and get back to booking calls. ← Previous [Testing Campaigns](/resources/testing-campaigns-23bf6d94771f805c9811db214ef8ba07) Next → [Scaling Winners](/resources/scaling-winners-23bf6d94771f80368e09c69494a20dd4) On this page

# Scaling Winners # **Scaling Winners** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Scaling = **sending more emails to more of the right people, while tracking what happens so you know what to do next.** That’s it. ✅ **NOT** diagnosing. ✅ **NOT** rewriting copy. ✅ **NOT** panicking over tiny dips. This page assumes: - You’ve been through **Know Your Numbers** and **Diagnosing Campaigns**. - You already have a base campaign hitting predictable results. If that’s not true → **stop here.** Fix that first.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know if you need to increase inboxes and when to hit your volume ✅ How to increase your TAM ✅ Track your results so scaling feels controlled, not random. ✅ Book more calls without guessing. --- ## **1. Max Your Current Sending Capacity First** ---
Scaling starts with **more of the same thing that’s already working**. Do **not** jump to new markets until this box is ticked.
--- ### **A. Check If You’re Actually Maxed Out**
1. Look at the **last 2–3 weeks of daily sends in your email sending tool.** 2. Are you hitting the volume of sends **per inbox, every day**? - Most founders are running at **50–70% of their current capacity** without realising it. Use the [**Outbound Math Calculator**](https://www.closingclientsgroup.com/outbound-math-calculator) → if you’re under target, **load existing inboxes fully before buying more.**
### **B. Add More Inboxes (Only When Maxed)** ---
- When you’ve hit full capacity → **then** add inboxes. - Follow your existing warm-up/ramp rules (you already know this from earlier sections). ⚠️ **If reply rates tank or bounce >2% while scaling:** Stop sending → go back to [**Diagnose and Fix Campaigns Fast**](/resources/diagnose-and-fix-campaigns-fast-23bf6d94771f80cd8429db9891d2687b)
--- ## **2. Increase Your TAM (More People to Email)** ---
Once you’ve maxed sending capacity, the only other lever is **more of the right people**. **Do it in this order:**
--- ### **A. Exhaust Your Core ICP First** --- Go deeper before going wider:
✅ **All company sizes** (2–5 person shops vs 10–20). ✅ **All job titles** (Owner, Head of Growth, Media Buyer). ✅ **All regions** (US, UK, Canada, Australia). Most founders leave **thousands of untouched leads** in their core ICP before chasing new markets.
### **B. Parallel Niches (Concentric Expansion)** --- Target industries with the **same pain & same problem** you already solve.
Examples: - Paid ads agencies → **SEO agencies**. - Coaches → **course creators**. - Local gyms → **chiropractors** or **physios**. **Ask:** “Who else has the exact same problem as my current clients?”
### **C. Geographic Expansion** --- If your offer works in English-speaking countries:
1. Start: **US → UK → Australia.** 2. You could then test **Europe** (You’d be surprised how many people reply to English emails). ⚠️ **Same ICP + New Region** is safer than jumping to a totally new niche.
### **D. Job Titles Expansion** --- Sometimes you’re emailing too narrowly.
✅ Founders ✅ CMOs / Marketing Managers ✅ Growth Leads If they feel the pain and can influence buying → **test them**.
### **E. Offer Variations (Only If Needed)** --- If you truly max out your ICP:
- **Lightly tweak your offer** to fit adjacent markets. - **Don’t rebuild your entire system.** Example: Booking calls for paid ads agencies → **tweak guarantee** slightly for SEO agencies.
## **3. Track While Scaling** ---
**Scaling without tracking = stupid** If you don’t track what you’re doing… Then you don’t know what you’re doing **We’ve built a campaign tracker for you** This tracker takes minutes to fill out and tells you what to send more of, what to kill, and when to stop.
### **Open the Tracker (Make a copy to add to your Google Drive)** 👉 [**CLICK HERE TO ACCESS THE CAMPAIGN TRACKER**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1VQkZhvdCZrZ1s3RBR8-P2-gptp4MSWtABLj-kkc40iM/copy) --- ### **Why Use It** --- - Stop guessing. Know exactly which angles, offers, and lists produce booked calls. - Spot what’s not working before it burns your domains. - Find winning patterns you can repeat every time you scale. --- ### **What to Log** --- - **Campaign name** - **Target audience** - **Emails sent** - **Replies & meetings booked** - **Notes** (offer, list source, or angle tested) - **A/B version** (if you’re testing variants) --- ### **How to Use It** --- 1. **Open the tracker** 2. **Look at the example rows** already inside. 3. **Replace them with your real data**. 4. To add new campaigns: - Right-click the whole table → **Copy** - Paste it below the last row → **Rename it** → start logging. 5. Update after **every campaign**, even if you only send 20 emails. --- ### **Why It Works** --- Most founders guess, change everything, and ruin good campaigns. The best cold emailers log their numbers, repeat what works, and scale calmly. Track every time you send your future self will thank you.
### ✅ Scaling done right is boring. Boring = booked calls. Be boring. Get results.

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Testing Campaigns # **Testing Campaigns** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most founders “test” by rewriting emails every three days. That’s not testing. $30K operators treat testing like building a machine: – One input changed at a time – Enough data to spot real patterns – Never moving on until they have clear winners If you don’t follow this in order, you’ll waste thousands of leads and still not know what works.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ See what proper testing actually looks like when done right ✅ Understand the full Sniper Testing flow (Macro → Micro → Follow-Up) ✅ Know which page to start with right now ## **Why Most People Get This Wrong** --- ❌ They test 500–1,000 leads → call it a winner ❌ Rewrite emails every week → no clean data ❌ Change follow-ups mid-test → can’t tell what actually worked ❌ Complain *“I can’t get consistent results” -* this is why. ## **What It Looks Like When Done Right** ---
This is how $30K/month operators test. Not by guessing. Not by throwing “new copy” at the wall every week. They follow a system. They treat testing like building a machine.
**Here’s what that looks like 👇** --- 1. **Start With Angles That Come From Real Market Insights** ✅ Pull angles from your GTM + Buyer Persona work ✅ Focus on Friction, Goal, Trigger, or Comparison angles (the 4 core types) ✅ Test all distinct angles - let the market pick the winner 2. **Prove the Market Cares (Macro Testing)** ✅ 2,000 leads per angle ✅ Kill obvious losers early (0.7% reply at 700 sends? Dead) ✅ Don’t chase perfection, just find the clear outperformer 3. **Refine the Winner (Micro Testing)** ✅ One variable at a time (CTA, hook, tone, or length) ✅ Keep testing until replies + booked calls stop improving 4. **Lock in the Follow-Up** ✅ Only test follow-ups *after* the winning initial email is set ✅ Test whether to stay on the same pain/outcome or agitate a new one ✅ Test value type (mini case study vs lead magnet vs logic breakdown) 5. **Scale Predictably** ✅ Only after all 3 stages outperform your old Base campaign do you “promote to Base” ✅ That’s why operators scale predictably while amateurs stay stuck guessing --- ### **The Sniper Testing Framework (High-Level Map)** | **Stage** | **Goal** | **Outcome** | Link | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Step 1: Macro Testing** | Find your best angle | 1–2 clear winning angles | [**Macro Testing – Find The Best Angle**](/resources/macro-testing-23bf6d94771f8039898ccef4758f3bbb) | | **Step 2: Micro Testing** | Fine-tune the winner | Highest-performing version of that angle | [**Micro Testing - Fine-Tune The Winner**](/resources/micro-testing-23bf6d94771f8049b903d80b1ea8e2b7) | | **Step 3: Follow-Up Testing** | Lock in the full 2-email sequence | Max booked calls before scaling | [**Follow-Up Testing - Lock In The Sequence**](/resources/follow-up-testing-23bf6d94771f807783b8cb3a67a2504c) | ## **The Sniper Testing Framework Step-By-Step** ---
Go through these steps in order 👇
## Step 1: Macro Testing – Find The Best Angle --- [Macro Testing](/resources/macro-testing-23bf6d94771f8039898ccef4758f3bbb) ## **Step 2: Micro Testing -** Fine-Tune The Winner --- [**Micro Testing**](/resources/micro-testing-23bf6d94771f8049b903d80b1ea8e2b7) ## **Step 3: Follow-Up Testing -** Lock In The Sequence --- [**Follow-Up Testing**](/resources/follow-up-testing-23bf6d94771f807783b8cb3a67a2504c) ← Previous [Test, Diagnose & Scale Campaigns](/resources/test-diagnose-scale-campaigns-23bf6d94771f803181a5e34d4df80072) Next → [Diagnose and Fix Campaigns Fast](/resources/diagnose-and-fix-campaigns-fast-23bf6d94771f80cd8429db9891d2687b) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.title: Follow-Up Testing
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Follow-Up Testing # **Follow-Up Testing** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Follow-Up Testing is NOT where you “get creative” – You already know the winning **initial email** – Now you’re proving which **follow-up** gets the most booked calls – Same discipline: one change at a time Skip this and you’ll scale with the wrong follow-up, leaving booked calls (and revenue) on the table.
## **Goal** --- Lock in the best **2-email sequence** (initial + follow-up) before scaling. Your only job here is to find the follow-up that turns **replies into booked calls** at the highest rate. --- ## **What Goes Into Follow-Up Testing** --- ✅ **ONLY after you have your final Micro winner** – No “testing” if the first email still isn’t proven. – You’re optimising conversion now, not reply rate. --- ## **What to Test** ---
The question you’re answering: 👉 ***What kind of follow-up gets the most booked calls?***
### **The 4 Key Follow-Up Variables (one at a time):** --- - [ ] **Angle Consistency vs New Angle** – Stick with same pain/outcome in initial email → add context – Or agitate a related pain / hint at new solution - [ ] **Value Type** – Mini case study vs logic breakdown vs lead magnet - [ ] **CTA Style** – Soft (“Happy to share if helpful?”) vs Hard (“Want me to show you how we did this for X?”) - [ ] **Length** – Short breakdown ( ~75 words) vs Context-rich (100–150 words) --- ## **What It Looks Like in Practice** ---
You’re applying the same Sniper process: ONE variable per variant → Kill losers fast → Pick the clear winner.
### **Example:** Your **initial email**: Friction angle (“Outbound hasn’t worked for you before”) Follow-Up split tests could look like this: | **Variant** | **What Changes** | **Rest of Email** | | --- | --- | --- | | **A** | Same angle, more context (“Here’s why 90% of agencies fail at outbound…”) | Everything else identical | | **B** | New pain angle (“The real issue isn’t leads, it’s - fixed this for 3 PR firms last month”) | Everything else identical | | **C** | Mini case study value (“We ran a 30-day sprint for X → 7 calls booked in 14 days”) | Everything else identical | | **D** | Hard CTA (“Want me to show you the exact process?”) | Everything else identical | ---
✅ Pick winner → ❌ Kill losers → 🔁 Repeat with next variable
--- ## **Rules to Run Follow-Up Tests** --- ✅ **Only 1 variable at a time** – No mixing (e.g., new angle + new CTA in the same variant). ✅ **~1,000 leads per variant** – Enough to see booked call patterns without burning your whole list. ✅ **Keep the same initial email (your Micro winner)** – You’re testing the **follow-up.** We want to know the best follow-up to match the best initial email. --- ## **Pick the Final Winner** --- ✅ Compare **reply → booked call ratio** side by side (not just reply rate). ✅ Drop any variant **25%+ worse than the best** after ~1,000 sends. ✅ Final winner = the sequence (initial + follow-up) that consistently drives the highest booked call rate. --- ## **Summary Checklist** --- - [ ] Start ONLY after Micro Testing winner is set - [ ] 1 variable tested per variant - [ ] Same initial email for all variants - [ ] 1,000 leads per variant - [ ] Kill any variant 25%+ worse than the best - [ ] Final winner = highest booked call ratio --- ## **Next Step** --- Once you’ve locked in the best sequence, and it consistently beats your base campaign. **IT BECOMES YOUR NEW BASE CAMPAIGN** That means: ✅ This becomes your default campaign for this market. ✅ Only after it outperforms your old Base consistently do you scale volume. ← Previous [Micro Testing](/resources/micro-testing-23bf6d94771f8049b903d80b1ea8e2b7) Next → [Testing Campaigns](/resources/testing-campaigns-23bf6d94771f805c9811db214ef8ba07) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.title: Macro Testing
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Macro Testing # Macro Testing ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Macro Testing is the only reliable way to find angles that scale. – One variable changes at a time – Minimum 2,000 sends per angle – Kill losers fast, double down on winners Skip this process and you’re guessing. That’s why you can’t repeat results.
## **Goal** --- Find the **angle** your market responds to most. This decides your entire outbound approach. (Angles come from your [Find Your Angles](/resources/find-your-angles-233f6d94771f80238c0cc1ef2fac6ec4) section.) --- ## **The 4-Step Macro Testing Process** --- ### **1: Set Up Your Tests** --- ✅ **One test = one initial email** – 5 angles = 5 separate split tests (A, B, C, D, E). – Each test is a different **angle** (pain vs outcome vs proof). --- ✅ **One follow-up for ALL variants** – You’re testing initial emails, not follow-ups. – Different follow-ups = dirty data (Angle A might only look good because Follow-Up E saved it). --- ✅ **Same send settings across all variants** – Same daily volume, same time of day, same list source. – Change nothing else. Every variable other than the initial email stays fixed. --- ### **Step 2: Send Enough Volume** --- ✅ **AT LEAST 2,000 leads per angle** Not 500. Not 1,000. **2,000. Why?**
I’ve lost count of how many angles looked like they would be losers at ~750 leads… then turned into winners at ~2,000. These tests determine your ***entire approach to this market*.** You can’t afford to half-ass it.
--- ### **Step 3: Pick the Winner** --- ✅ Compare all angles **side by side** → **best reply → positive reply ratio wins**. ✅ Don’t chase perfection. You’re looking for the **clearest outperformer**. ✅ If 2–3 are close → keep them all. You’ll **refine each one separately** in Micro Testing to see which can be pushed to the top. --- ### **Step 4: Kill Rule** --- ❌ **Under 0.7% reply at ~750 sends while others hit 1.2–1.5%+?** **Kill it.** Don’t waste 2,000 leads on obvious losers. --- ## ✅ **Summary Checklist (Print This)** --- - [ ] One variable per test (initial email only) - [ ] Same follow-up + same send settings across all variants - [ ] 2,000 sends per angle (unless killed early) - [ ] Kill <0.7% reply at ~750 sends if others are 1.2–1.5%+ - [ ] Promote only 1–2 winners to Micro Testing ## **Next Step** --- Once you’ve got 1–2 clear winning angles, move to [**Micro Testing – Fine-Tune The Winner**](/resources/micro-testing-23bf6d94771f8049b903d80b1ea8e2b7). That’s where you’ll start squeezing every extra % out of the winning angle. O**ne variable at a time** (CTA, hook, tone, or length). ← Previous [Testing Campaigns](/resources/testing-campaigns-23bf6d94771f805c9811db214ef8ba07) Next → [Micro Testing](/resources/micro-testing-23bf6d94771f8049b903d80b1ea8e2b7) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.title: Micro Testing
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Micro Testing # **Micro Testing** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Micro Testing is where you squeeze every last % out of your best angle. – No new ideas. Only refinement. – One change per variant. Skip this process and you’ll scale a mediocre version of your angle instead of the one that could double your booked calls.
## **Goal** --- Take the strongest Macro angle(s) and refine them until you have **one clear, proven winner**. You’re not “trying new stuff” here. You’re improving positioning. --- ## **Which Angles Go Into Micro** --- ✅ **Only promote the strongest Macro performers** – **1 clear winner?** Start refining it immediately. – **2–3 close winners?** Keep them all. Test side-by-side to find the best overall. – Kill everything else. --- ## **What to Test** ---
You’re testing **execution, not angle**. The market already told you the **what** — now you find the best way to say it.
### **Example:**
Macro winner = ***Friction angle*** (“Outbound hasn’t worked for you before”) Micro = Test **how** you frame that: - Lead with blunt pain vs tease the alternative first - Soft invite vs hard “let me show you how we fixed this for X” CTA
### **The Main Variables We Suggest To Test (one at a time):** --- - [ ] **Subject Line:** Personal vs Benefit-led vs internal email - [ ] **Opening Hook:** Pain-first vs Outcome-first - [ ] **CTA:** Soft interest (“Worth exploring?”) vs Hard ask (“Want me to show you?”) vs Lead Magnet vs Book a call - [ ] **Offer Framing:** Direct pitch vs “no-brainer” lead magnet (guide, teardown) - [ ] **Tone:** Consultative vs Authoritative - [ ] **Length:** Short punchy (40 words) vs Context-heavy (70–80 words) - [ ] **P.S:** With vs Without --- ## **What It Looks Like in Practice** ---
You’re using the same approach to your Macro tests. It’s just now you already know the best angle to use.
### **Example:** Macro winner = ***Friction angle*** (as mentioned above) Here’s how the Micro split tests could look: | **Variant** | **What Changes** | **Rest Of Email** | | --- | --- | --- | | **A** | Hard CTA (“Want me to show you how we fixed this for X?”) | Stays EXACTLY the same. ONLY this one variable changes | | **B** | Soft CTA (“Interested?”) | Stays EXACTLY the same. ONLY this one variable changes | | **C** | Lead Magnet CTA (“Happy to send you the teardown we used for X?”) | Stays EXACTLY the same. ONLY this one variable changes | | **D** | Book a call - (”Do you have 20minutes next Tuesday?”) | Stays EXACTLY the same. ONLY this one variable changes | **That’s it.**
✅ Pick winner → ❌ Kill losers → 🔁 Repeat with next variable (subject line, tone, etc.)
## **Rules to Run Micro Tests** --- ✅ **One variable at a time per variant** – Change the subject line only → everything else stays the same. – You’re finding *what actually moves the needle*. ✅ ~**1,000 leads per variant** – Enough to see clear patterns without burning through your entire list. ✅ **Same follow-up for all variants** – You’re still testing initial emails, not sequences. --- ## **Pick the Final Winner** --- ✅ Compare reply → positive reply ratio side by side. ✅ Drop any variant **25%+ worse than the best** after ~1,000 sends. ✅ Final winner = the variant that consistently holds the best reply → **booked call** ratio. --- ## **Summary Checklist (Print This)** --- - [ ] 1 variable changed per variant - [ ] Same follow-up + send settings for all variants - [ ] 1,000 leads per variant - [ ] Kill any variant 25%+ worse than the best - [ ] Only promote 1 final winner to Follow-Up Testing --- ## **Next Step** Once you have your final winner, move to [**Follow-Up Testing – Lock In The Sequence**](/resources/follow-up-testing-23bf6d94771f807783b8cb3a67a2504c). That’s where you build the full 2-email sequence to **maximise booked calls before scaling**. ← Previous [Macro Testing](/resources/macro-testing-23bf6d94771f8039898ccef4758f3bbb) Next → [Follow Up Testing](/resources/follow-up-testing-23bf6d94771f807783b8cb3a67a2504c) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/client.content:
# Find Your Angles # Find Your Angles ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most founders think their cold emails aren’t working because of bad copy. Wrong. It’s because they didn’t develop an angle based on real insights. If a buyer has already: - Tried another agency and got burned - Seen 15 other “we’ll get you more demos” emails this week - Wasted $8K on SDRs who didn’t convert …and then receive a message saying: > “We help SaaS founders book more calls through cold outbound.” > Then you can very easily understand why they wouldn’t respond to this. At $5K, you can afford to guess. At $30K, every angle has to hit first time or you’re burning cash and time. This page forces you to stop guessing and will show you how to finally fix this, forever.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ 5–10 cold email angles pulled directly from real buyer tension ✅ Each one tied to a specific pain, goal, trigger, or cost ✅ Know EXACTLY what campaign to create to get results ✅ A way to finally stop sounding like everyone else ✅ You will learn how to write better emails by simply knowing how to **say what your buyer is already thinking.** ## 1. What Is an Angle? --- An **angle** is the approach you use to maximise resonance with prospects. It's how you show them: **"I understand you and I'm not guessing."** It's not your offer. It's not your value prop. It's not a clever hook. **It's the specific buyer situation you're targeting** to prove you actually get what they're dealing with. ### An angle is: - The entry point that makes your message matter to them personally - Based on real buyer intelligence you gathered in MIRROR - What separates you from the 15 other "we'll get you more demos" emails they got this week ### An angle is not: - A subject line trick - A benefit statement - Something you brainstormed because it "sounds good"
**Example:** **Without an angle:** "We help SaaS founders get more qualified demos" **With an angle:** "Stuck at $3M ARR for 6 months while competitors scale past you?" The second one proves you understand their specific situation. The first one could be sent to anyone.
--- ## 2. Why Most People Get Angles Wrong **(Even When They Know the Buyer)** --- You know your buyer: - They’ve worked with other agencies and got no results - Maybe they’ve tried running your service in house and got no results - They get 10+ cold emails a week with a similar offer to yours Yet founders will still write emails like they’re the first one to reach out to them
**“We help ecom brands increase revenue from their email list.”**
Here’s how the prospect actually reads this:
**“We’re like everyone else, please delete me.”**
### Specific example I see ALL the time: Let’s say you sell email marketing to ecom brands. You know they either: - Have someone in-house managing this - Already pay an agency to do this But people STILL send:
**“If we could help you generate more from your emails…”**
Instead of:
**“You’re already managing this in-house or have an agency running it for you. But when’s the last time you audited their performance? If we could outperform….”**
This is why they don’t win --- ## 3. The 4 Types Of Angles --- All angles will fall broadly into one of these 4 categories 👇 | **Angle Type** | **What It Targets** | **Example** | **Why It Works** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **FRICTION** | Pain they’re going thorugh/What they've tried that didn't work | "Tried outbound but couldn't get it to work? Tried agencies but no results?” | Validates their frustration | | **GOAL** | What they want but can't get | "Need to grow but can't do it with just inbound and referrals anymore?” | Acknowledges ambition + constraint | | **TRIGGER** | Something that just happened | "Hiring for SDRs? Running ads? Just raised funding?” | Perfect timing relevance | | **COMPARISON** | They're already doing something, you can do it better | "I know you're already doing email marketing in-house, but let me show you how it could be done better” | Respects current efforts + positions superiority | --- ## 4. How To Find Your Angles --- You use the buyer persona MIRROR framework research you did in the buyer persona section. Go back through this framework: - **M**apped out the role - **I**dentified their goals and frictions - **R**evealed their current reality - **R**ecognized the stakes (what happens if they don't fix this) - **O**bserved their words (how they speak) - **R**eflected it back (developed messaging around this) ### **This is the unlock that allows you to scale to $30K+ month.** --- This is where $5K-$15K/month agencies get it wrong. They think they just need the latest Clay automations to mention something about their LinkedIn profile. Wrong. The pros of cold outreach know they need to make the prospect say **"fuck, that's me."** ### Example: Jake's MIRROR Breakdown --- **Jake:** Founder of a $3M/year CRM platform. Stuck at $3M for the past 6 months. Tried 2 agencies and couldn't get results. Tried outbound themselves. Inbound is no longer enough to grow their business. They know they need to make outbound work, just need to find a way to actually do it and have someone they can trust to get results for them, but right now don't believe really anyone can. Feels frustrated, stuck and a bit lost. ### Jake's Extracted Angles: - "Stuck at $3M/year - we helped competitor do X" - "Reliant upon inbound and referrals and now it's not enough to generate the growth you need?" - "Tried agencies and they've not been able to get results?" - "Can’t afford to hire a sales team but need the results they generate?" Each angle comes directly from his MIRROR research and targets a specific emotional state he's experiencing. ### BONUS: Need help pulling the angles? Use this ChatGPT prompt If you’ve done your MIRROR persona but the angles still aren’t hitting, paste this into ChatGPT: ``` Based on this buyer persona, give me: - 3 Friction angles - 3 Goal angles - 3 Trigger angles - 3 Comparison angles Each should be 1 line I could open a cold email with. Make them reflect real tension, not benefits or features. Context: Angle Types (Do NOT copy these — they are examples only) Angle type: Friction What it targets: Pain they’re going through / what they’ve tried that didn’t work Example: “Tried outbound but couldn’t get it to work? Tried agencies but no results?” Why it works: Validates their frustration Angle type: Goal What it targets: What they want but can’t get yet Example: “Need to grow but can’t do it with just inbound and referrals anymore?” Why it works: Acknowledges ambition + constraint Angle type: Trigger What it targets: Something that just happened (visible action/event) Example: “Hiring for SDRs? Running ads? Just raised funding?” Why it works: Perfect timing relevance Angle type: Comparison What it targets: They’re already doing something, but you can do it better Example: “I know you’re already doing email marketing in-house, but let me show you how it could be done better” Why it works: Respects current efforts + positions superiority Here’s the buyer persona: [PASTE HERE] ``` --- ## 5. Weak vs Strong Angles: | **Weak Angle** | **Why It Fails** | **Strong Angle** | **Why It Works** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | “Hey, saw you’re in SaaS…” | Generic. No specificity. | “SaaS founders at $3M ARR often hit the inbound plateau.” | Sharp, timely, and founder-specific | | “Looking for a lead gen partner?” | Sounds like a cold email. | “Sick of leadgen agencies who can’t get results, but need the results they promise?” | Punchy, visceral, and emotionally true | | “Interested in 2–3 more clients?” | Vague, evergreen ask. | “3 agencies added 10+ clients without changing a single thing.” | Creates curiosity + specific gain | ### How weak angles affect your campaigns: | **Responses...** | **The real issue is...** | **Fix it by...** | | --- | --- | --- | | “Not interested” | Angle is too vague | Tie it to a *friction* or *trigger* they’re facing now | | “Already tried that” | You’re leading with your solution | Start with what *didn’t work,* not what you do | | No reply at all | No tension, question, or sharp insight | Use a *comparison* or contrarian/pattern interupt statement |
ℹ️ **Remember:** Replies are feedback. And we can use this feedback to help inform the angles we use.
--- ## 6. Now You Have Your Angles - What Do You Do With Them? ---
ℹ️ **Here's what separates $5K agencies from $30K agencies.**
To hit $30K+ months, decisions need to be based on feedback and not on what you think or how you feel. Now you have your angles, based on REAL insights. You need to test them fast so you know angles Jake (example used above) and other prospects like him, actually respond best to.
**GOAL:** find a winning angle as quickly as possible so you can scale campaigns and generate results ASAP.
### Here’s how you do it: --- 1. **Build one test campaign:** With 5 (more if you have more angles) initial email variations, each using a different angle. **Example:** - **Test A:** “Stuck at $3M/year?” - **Test B:** “Still reliant on inbound?” - **Test C:** “Tried agencies and still no results?” - **Test D:** “Need the results of a sales team without the cost?” - **Test E:** A combined angle that mixes the strongest tension points. 2. **Send to prospects** and track performance and replies closely. 3. **Watch for patterns.** Which angle gets the most replies? Which replies give the clearest feedback about what buyers care about? 4. **Double down on what hits.** Kill the rest. Scale only the angles that actually got traction.
We’ll go deeper into testing in the upcoming sections. For now, **finish extracting your angles so you can move to this step.**
## 7. Exercise: Extract Your Angles ---
**Do this right now 👇**
### Step 1: Go Through Your MIRROR Framework Open your completed MIRROR persona and pull all insights from each section: - **Map the Role:** What pressures, responsibilities, daily struggles? - **Identify Goals + Frictions:** What they want vs. what's blocking them - **Reveal Current Reality:** What they've tried that failed or isn't working - **Recognize Stakes:** What happens if they don't fix this - **Observe Their Words:** How they actually speak about their problems - **Reflect It Back:** The messaging themes you identified ### **Step 2: Pull Your Angles** Extract 1–2 angles for each category: - **Friction →** “Tried X? Still Y?” - **Goal →** “Want X without Y?” - **Trigger →** “Saw you’re X…” - **Comparison →** “Paying X for Y?” You should now have 5–7 angles that sound like you’ve been sitting in their meetings. ### **Step 3: Turn Them Into Campaign Tests** Write out what campaign tests you would create with these angles: - **Test A:** [Friction angle] - **Test B:** [Goal angle] - **Test C:** [Trigger angle] - **Test D:** [Comparison angle] - **Test E:** [Combined test using all angles] Make sure you have a test for each individual angle type, plus one test that combines all angles into a single message.
**⚠️ Don't move forward until you have 5 testable angles extracted from your MIRROR research.**

# Think Like a Buyer # Think Like a Buyer ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** - At $5K/month you guess what people might want. At $30K you reverse-engineer exactly what they need to hear, do, and experience You get the same cold emails every week and ignore them, yet you send the exact same ones to your prospects. - You hate long onboarding processes when you buy something, yet you make your clients go through it, before they can get started. - **This is the same founder lens you installed in the $30K Mentality section — we're now applying it to outbound, delivery, and everything your buyers experience.**
## What This Page Will Help You Do By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Stop guessing and start reverse-engineering everything from buyer reality ✅ Instantly spot why your emails, onboarding, and delivery aren’t optimised ✅ Build client systems that make buyers think: "Finally, these guys get it." ✅ Start weaponising every single buyer interaction for faster growth --- ## 1. Why You're Stuck (And Don't Even Realise It) ---
‼ You think you're thinking like a buyer. You're not.
**Here's the harsh truth:** - You delete every "We help agencies book more calls" email. → Then you send the same shit to your own prospects. - You hate long onboarding when you buy something. → Then you make clients sit through hour-long videos before they can start. - You want fast results when you buy. → Then you design delivery that slows your own clients down. At $5K/month, you can get away with this. But you can’t hit $30K/month doing this. --- ## 2. The 10-Minute Gut-Punch Exercise (Do This Now) ---
Seriously. Do this now 👇
### Step 1: Open your inbox - Look at the last 10 cold emails you ignored. Write down WHY you ignored them. - Now compare them to the last 10 emails you sent. → Be honest: are they basically the same? ### Step 2: List the last 3 services YOU bought (could be inbox services etc) - What made you pull the trigger fast? What pissed you off about the experience? - Now check: → Are you doing the same things you hated to YOUR clients? ### Step 3: Brutal honesty check - Would YOU buy from you if you got your own pitch email? Why or why not? - If you feel even slightly sick writing these answers, good. That's your $30K unlock. --- ## 3. The $30K Operator Difference ---
**$5K operators ask:** "What shall I do or say?" **$30K operators ask:** "If I were my buyer, What would I want to see/experience?”
### $30K Operators: ✅ Start from buyer's current reality & urgency ✅ Watch what they ACTUALLY respond to in their own inbox ✅ Strip systems back to only what buyers need fastest ✅ Use language that's already made YOU stop scrolling ✅ Fix delivery based on buyer frustrations, not their ego ✅ Weaponise every buyer interaction for competitive advantage ### $5K Operators: ❌ Guess what "sounds good" ❌ Build "professional looking systems" buyers don't care about ❌ Use business-y language that feels safe but gets ignored ❌ Add features they think are impressive ❌ Ignore buyer feedback because "they don't understand" --- ## 4. Real Example: Onboarding Disaster → Buyer-First Fix ---
Someone I was recently consulting "improved" his onboarding: Nicely edited videos. Detailed step-by-step guide. Was super happy with it.
**The result?** - Clients took LONGER to onboard - He didn’t get paid as fast (charged per call) - Results were delayed - Both sides got frustrated
**Why?** He built onboarding for himself, not for the buyer. **The buyer's reality was:** *"I just signed a deal. I need leads fast. I'm busy, frustrated, juggling 20 things. Just tell me the 3 things I absolutely need to do to get started."* We reversed engineered his onboarding to THINK like they buyer and stripped it back to the bare minimum **Result:** Clients onboarded immediately, results came faster, and he got paid sooner.
--- ## 5. Weaponising Your Buyer Experience ---
### $30K operators weaponise EVERY buyer interaction.
### **Here's how:** --- - **Emails you open vs. ignore** → Only write angles that made YOU stop scrolling. - **Objections on sales calls** → Address them BEFORE the call next time. - **Onboarding confusion** → If 3 clients ask the same question, FIX the process. - **Client excitement vs. complaints** → Sell ONLY what they celebrate, not what you think is impressive. - **Features they never mention/use** → Stop selling them/promoting them - **Delivery reactions** → If they love speed, strip away thoroughness that slows you down. --- ### 👊 **You're Your Own Case Study** --- If you wouldn't respond to your own cold email If you'd hate your own onboarding process If you'd be frustrated by your own delivery speed Why wouldn’t buyers be? The only difference between you and $30K a month? Stop building for yourself. Start building for your buyers. --- ![ChatGPT Image Jul 15, 2025, 12_35_15 PM.png](Think%20Like%20a%20Buyer/ChatGPT_Image_Jul_15_2025_12_35_15_PM.png) ## 6. The $30K Operator Checklist ---
Print this out 👇
✅ I send NO email I wouldn't personally reply to ✅ My onboarding gets clients results FASTER than they expect ✅ I can list 3 buyer frustrations I've removed in the past 30 days ✅ Every objection I hear is addressed BEFORE the sales call ✅ I track buyer reactions weekly and adjust immediately Miss even 1? You're still guessing. --- ## 7. Your Next Step (Choose ONE This Week) ---
Don't try to fix everything. Pick the ONE that will impact your revenue fastest:
- **If your reply rates suck:** Rewrite your outbound using ONLY angles that made YOU reply to emails - **If onboarding takes forever:** Strip ONE step from onboarding that slows buyers down - **If clients always complain about the same thing:** Fix ONE thing clients always ask questions about - **If sales calls feel like you're pushing uphill:** Address the #1 objection you hear BEFORE the call
Do this every week, and you won't stay stuck for long.
