# Write Like a Pro # Write Like a Pro ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You've done all the GTM work: ICP, persona, angles, buyer thinking but you're still probably thinking "what do I actually write?" If this is true, then this page is going to kill that thought so going forward you will know exactly what to write and when best to write it.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know the foundation rules of writing cold emails like a pro ✅ How to avoid the 3 mistakes that kill 90% of campaigns ✅ Learn the direct response frameworks that put you in the top 1% ✅ Sell the CTA instead of just asking for time ✅ Write emails that make prospects think "Fuck….these guys know what they’re doing." --- ## Writing Like A Pro - Cold Email Best Practices ---
You've already learned how to build compelling offers and extract angles from your GTM work. Now here's how to structure them in emails that actually get opened, read, and replied to. These are the tactical execution rules that apply regardless of which framework you use.
### Subject Line Rules: --- - Max 5 words - Use {{FirstName}} or {{CompanyName}} (not both) - Mimic internal emails - subtle questions, don't sell directly - Example: ❌ "Want to fix your lead problem?" ✅ "{{CompanyName}} inbound lead" ### Email Content Rules: --- - **50-125 words maximum** - **3rd-grade reading level** - short sentences, simple words - **Focus on what they gain, not what you do** - **No formatting** - no bullet points, bold text, or italics - **Clear structure:** body → thanks → P.S. ### Number Formatting: --- - **Use abbreviated formats:** "1M" not "1 million", "5K" not "5,000" ### Spam Trigger Words to Avoid: --- - **Never use:** "Free," "guaranteed," "risk-free," currency symbols ($, £, €) - **No excessive punctuation:** !!!, ALL CAPS, excessive dots (...) ### Technical Best Practices: - **Don't use free email providers** (@gmail.com, @yahoo.com) - **Include unsubscribe/opt-out option** - **First line should prove you understand them/can connect**
### 🧪 The 7-Second Test: Before sending, ask: Can they understand it in 7 seconds? Does it sound like an internal email? Would I reply to this?
**ℹ️ Remember:** These rules apply regardless of which framework you use. The framework determines your structure. These rules determine your execution.
## 3 Biggest Mistakes That Pro’s Avoid ---
Even with great angles and buyer intel, most founders kill their campaigns with these 3 mistakes:
### **Mistake 1: Writing Like a Salesperson** - ❌ "We're a leading provider of innovative solutions that help businesses optimise their sales processes..." - ✅ "Revenue been stuck for the last 6 months? We helped 17 other CRM founders break through to $8M in just 9 months." ### **Mistake 2: Generic Subject Lines That Scream "Sales Email"** - ❌ "Increase Your Revenue by 300% Today!" - ✅ "{{FirstName}} question" ### **Mistake 3: Asking for Time Instead of Selling Value** - ❌ "Can we schedule a 30-minute call to discuss your needs?" - ✅ "In 15-minutes we could provide you with [outcome]. Are you free next Tuesday at 10am EST?"
❌ They sound like every other agency instead of someone who actually understands their buyer's situation. ✅ Use your angles and buyer intel to prove you get their specific reality.
## Direct Response Copy Frameworks ---
ℹ️ This is where it all comes together. You've built your ICP, created your buyer persona, extracted your angles, and learned to think like a buyer. Now you'll never have to guess what to write again. These 4 frameworks take your buyer research and turn it into email structures that get replies.
### The 4 Frameworks That Make You Write Like A Pro: --- 1. **PAS (Problem-Agitation-Solution)** - Validates pain → agitates said pain → shows solution 2. **AIDA (Attention-Interest-Desire-Action)** - Grabs attention → builds interest → creates desire → demands action 3. **Before/After/Bridge** - Shows current state → desired outcome → your solution as the bridge 4. **Curiosity Loop** - Pattern interrupt → tease value → close loop with CTA
### **💭 Why these 4?** They cover every psychological trigger your buyer experiences: frustration (PAS), desire (AIDA), transformation (Before/After/Bridge), and curiosity (Curiosity Loop). Master these and you can structure any angle into a compelling email. Each framework triggers different psychological responses and works best with specific types of angles.
--- ## How to Know Which Framework to Use ---
Here's the simple decision tree that tells you exactly which framework to pick based on your angle type.
### The Decision Tree: --- ![ChatGPT Image Jul 16, 2025, 12_33_49 PM.png](Write%20Like%20a%20Pro/ChatGPT_Image_Jul_16_2025_12_33_49_PM.png)
### Why This Matching Works: **Friction angles** need validation → **PAS** validates their pain and offers relief **Goal angles** need desire building → **AIDA** creates want for the outcome **Trigger angles** need urgency → **Curiosity Loop** leverages perfect timing **Comparison angles** need transformation clarity → **Before/After/Bridge** shows the journey
## The 4 Frameworks In Practice ---
ℹ️ Now you'll see each framework in action using Jake (the CRM platform founder) as our consistent example. This shows you how the same buyer gets different treatments based on which angle and framework you choose.
### Jake's Buyer Persona Reminder: ---
**Jake:** CRM founder, $3M ARR. Stuck at same MRR 6+ months, inbound has stagnated. Tried 2 agencies, both overpromised and underdelivered. Wants growth, can't afford sales team yet.
--- ### **1. PAS (Problem-Agitation-Solution)** ---
**Use when:** They know the problem and have tried solutions that failed **Jake's angle:** Friction - "Tried 2 agencies, still stuck at $3M"
**Email example:**
*Hi Jake, tried lead generation agencies and even outbound in-house but still no results? (pain) Our CRM platform clients have told us the struggle they faced before working with us on how they just couldn’t scale to hit growth targets and found it almost impossible to find a trusted partner. (Agitate)* *We helped another CRM founder break through to 8M/year in 9 months after not being able to crack outbound. We guarantee 5 qualified and attending sales calls within 30 days or you don’t pay. (Solution) Are you free on Tuesday at 10am? Thanks*
--- ### **2. AIDA (Attention-Interest-Desire-Action)**
**Use when:** They want something but need desire built **Jake's angle:** Goal - "Want growth without hiring sales team"
**Email example:**
*Hi Jake, Want to hit 8M ARR without hiring a sales team? (Attention)* *We just helped another CRM founder scale from 3M to 8M in 9 months using our outbound system. (Interest)* *Imagine having predictable deals every month without managing reps, training costs, or hiring headaches. (Desire)* *Worth exploring how we did it? (Action)*
--- ### **3. Curiosity Loop (Pattern Interrupt-Tease-CTA)**
**Use when:** They're skeptical or hard to reach **Jake's angle:** Trigger - "Just saw you're hiring SDRs"
**Email example:**
*Hi Jake, Saw you're hiring SDRs. Most 3M/year CRM founders make the same mistake when scaling outbound. (Pattern Interrupt)* *We helped another founder avoid it and hit 8M in 14 months instead of burning through reps. (Tease)* *Curious what the mistake was? (CTA)*
--- ### **4. Before/After/Bridge (Current-Desired-Solution)**
**Use when:** They can clearly visualize the transformation **Jake's angle:** Comparison - "Stuck at $3M vs. competitors growing"
**Email example:**
*Hi Jake, From: Stuck at 3M with because inbound and referrals are no longer enough.* *To: 8M ARR with predictable outbound growth.* *How? A proven system that works without you managing every detail.* *Worth a call to see if we can get you there?*
--- ### **Key insight:**
🔑 Same buyer, same offer, but different psychological triggers based on the angle and framework combination. This is why the decision tree works - it matches the right psychology to the right buyer state.
## CTA Psychology (Plug & Play) ---
### What CTAs Actually Are CTAs are the moment your email lives or dies. This is where perfect emails with great angles go to die because founders don't know how to sell the next step.
### Why Most CTAs Fail ---
Most founders think if something is "valuable," people will automatically want it. Wrong.
**Example:**
❌ **Shit CTA:** "Can I show you the emails we used to book meetings for CRM platforms?" ✅ **10x Better CTA:** "We have the exact campaign strategy, meetings booked, campaign angles, company responses and emails we used to finally solve outbound for 19 CRM platforms generating $2.3M within 90 days. Takes 3 minutes to review."
### Why the Second One Works: It's not just about value. It's about: - How valuable is it vs. how much effort will it take me? - Is the outcome promised enough to justify that effort? - Do I believe the outcome? **The real equation: $2.3M outcome for 3 minutes = no-brainer** ### The Asset CTA Method (Start Here) --- Asset CTAs convert highest because they: - Prove value upfront - No calendar commitment fear - Specific and tangible **Formula:** Specific asset + outcome + time to consume ### Jake Example: ---
❌ "Want to hop on a call about outbound?" ✅ "We have the exact campaign playbook, email templates, and response analysis we used to get another CRM founder from $3M to $8M in 9 months. Takes 4 minutes to review. Shall we send it?"
### When to Use Asset vs. Meeting CTAs --- **Simple decision tree:** ![Untitled design.png](Write%20Like%20a%20Pro/Untitled_design.png) ### Framework-Specific CTA Formulas --- **PAS:** "Worth reviewing the [asset] that solved this for [similar company]?" **AIDA:** "Want the [asset] that delivered [specific result]?" **Curiosity:** "Curious about the [asset] that helped them avoid this?" **Before/After:** "Want to see the [asset] that got [company] from [before] to [after]?" ### RULE: Never Book Calls Like This ---
❌ **Call link method:** "Here's my link to book a call" (35% booking rate) ✅ **Manual booking:** "Are you free Thursday at 2pm or Friday at 11am?" (85% booking rate) **Why manual works:** Removes friction, feels personal, gives them choice without overwhelming options.
### The 3-Second Test --- Before sending, ask: - Does this sell the value of saying yes? - Is the effort crystal clear? - Would I respond to this?
**Remember:** You're not asking for time. You're selling why the time is worth it.
## Your 10-Minute Write Like A Pro Challenge ---
🚨 Don’t move to the next section until you complete this. Unless you don’t want to win.
**Step 1:** Use YOUR buyer persona + YOUR extracted angle + the matching framework **Step 2:** Write the email using everything from this page **Step 3:** Post your draft in the group for feedback **OR** **Alternative:** Use the email writing tool in the next section with all your inputs, then post that result in the group for feedback. **The goal:** Get one working email draft using your actual buyer intel and angles

# Master Your Inbox (Turn Replies Into Booked Calls) # Master Your Inbox (Turn Replies Into Booked Calls) ---
🚨**QUICK NOTE:** Replies aren’t the win. Booked calls are. This section installs the system that turns every reply into a clear, qualified next step.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this section, you’ll: ✅ See and respond to every reply quickly without living in your inbox ✅ Qualify leads in under 10 seconds using a simple filter ✅ Respond with the right message for every reply type ✅ Book more calls with high-converting CTAs ✅ Take control of vague replies and get calls booked ## **1. Never Miss a Reply (Automation)** --- [**Never Miss a Reply (Automation)**](Master%20Your%20Inbox%20(Turn%20Replies%20Into%20Booked%20Calls)/Never%20Miss%20a%20Reply%20(Automation)%20241f6d94771f8090bc67d8e271663692.md) ## **2. # Delegate Your Inbox (Inbox Manager Training) # **Delegate Your Inbox (Inbox Manager Training)** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Your job is to try and turn every reply into a booked call in <5 min.
## 1. KPIs | Metric | Target | Why It Matters | | --- | --- | --- | | **Time‑to‑Lead** | **< 5 min** from Slack alert to first reply | 100× higher connect rate vs. 30‑min+ replies | | **Booked‑Call Rate** | **≥ 20 %** of positive replies | Shows we’re converting interest, not chatting | | **Response Accuracy** | **≥ 95 %** triage decisions match SOP | Keeps quality high at speed | Hit these numbers → you’re winning. --- ## 2. Alerts & Tool Access --- **How you’ll see every reply instantly** - **Slack** → `#inbox‑alerts` (push ON mobile + desktop) - **Master Inbox** → all campaign emails auto‑forward here - **Sequencer Inbox** (Instantly / SmartLead) → backup view - **Calendar** → permission to book calls etc - **CRM / Sheet** → log bookings + notes --- ## 3. 5‑Minute Response Loop 1. **Slack ping lands** → start stopwatch. 2. **10‑sec triage** → Green, Yellow, or Red (see below). 3. **Reply** using the correct 3‑line framework. 4. **Propose 2 call times** in prospect’s timezone. 5. **Book the call** when they confirm → send invite. 6. **Mark Slack message ✅** + log outcome in CRM. Repeat. Clock resets on every new alert. --- ## 4. Triage & Playbook ### Green / Yellow / Red Map | Colour | What It Really Means | Action | 3‑Line Framework | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | 🟢 **Green** | Wants next step / info | Reply → Book call now | “Can walk you through how we did it. Free Tue 2 pm or Wed 10 am—work?” | | 🟡 **Yellow** | Fence‑sitting / needs proof | Clarify, drop screenshot, re‑ask | “Totally get it. Quick Q—are you running paid ads now? If yes, can show you how we 2× leads. Free Tue 2 pm or Wed 10 am?” | | 🔴 **Red** | Not interested / spam | Exit cleanly | “All good—no more emails. Appreciate the reply.” | When in doubt → treat as Yellow and ask one clarifying question. --- ## 5. Golden Rules 1. **No booking links** unless they ask. 2. **≤ 50 words** per email—clear > clever. 3. **Mirror their language** (tone & keywords). 4. **Never quote price** or deep‑explain offer. Goal = earn the call. 5. **Stop only after a clear Yes or No.** --- ← Previous [Get the Call Booked](Get%20the%20Call%20Booked%20(CTA%20+%20Confirmation)%20241f6d94771f80b6846de8389e093bfd.md) Next → [Master Your Inbox](../Master%20Your%20Inbox%20(Turn%20Replies%20Into%20Booked%20Calls)%20241f6d94771f8001a2eecc078dc738ff.md) On this page

# Get the Call Booked (CTA + Confirmation) # **Get the Call Booked (CTA + Confirmation)** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This page shows you how to propose times, confirm properly, and handle vague replies to get more calls booked.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Send call CTAs that convert ✅ Book confirmed calls without ever sending a link ✅ Handle vague or soft responses like “next week works” or “sure” ✅ Get more calls booked ## **Why Most Founders Get This Wrong** --- - **They send a link too early.** Looks lazy. Creates decision fatigue. Gets ignored. - **They use weak CTAs.** “Hop on a call?” = no urgency, no clarity, no reply. - **They don’t take control.** Vague replies = no follow-up plan. - **They fear being presumptive.** So they over-soften the ask and lose the lead. > You’re not forcing them. You’re leading them. If they replied, you’ve earned that. > --- ## **How to Book the Call (Without Links)** --- ### **Step 1: Use a Manual CTA (Always Propose 2 Times)** > Never ask “Would you like to hop on a call?” > > > Instead, offer two specific times that match your calendar. > ✅ Example: > “Can walk you through how we did it. > > > Free Tuesday at 2pm or Wednesday at 10am?” > 🧠 Why this works: - Shows leadership - Removes friction - Signals you’re a real operator, not a calendar link spammer --- ### **Step 2: Once They Confirm - Book It Yourself** > Don’t say “Great, here’s my link to book” > > > Just book the call. > ✅ Example: > “Perfect. I’ve booked you in for Wednesday at 10am (their time). > > > You’ll have received a calendar invite. > > Looking forward to speaking then.” > 🔁 Why this works: - Feels white-glove - Reduces drop-off - Reinforces commitment --- ### **Step 3: If They’re Vague - Default, Then Offer Options** > If they say “next week works” or “sure”... > ✅ Example: > “Great - I’ve booked you in for Wednesday at 10am (your time). > > > If that doesn’t work, just reply and pick one of these: > > - Tuesday 2pm > - Thursday 11am > > Otherwise, see you then.” > 🧠 Why this works: - Assumes the close - Gives them an easy way to change it - Higher show-up rate than leaving it open-ended --- ## **Vague Reply Handling Cheatsheet** | **Reply** | **What To Do** | | --- | --- | | “Next week works” | Book default time + give 2 backups | | “Sounds good” | Assume acceptance → book → confirm + calendar invite | | “Yeah sure, send a link” | Don’t send a link - offer 2 times instead | | “What’s your calendar?” | Flip it: “Free Tue 2pm or Wed 10am - either work for you?” | | “Can I see some info first?” | Use Evaluating reply CTA (see previous page), then CTA after proof is shared | --- ## **Pro-Level Booking Rules** --- 1. **No booking links.** You’re not a chatbot. Propose times. Book manually. 2. **Always confirm the time + calendar invite.** Assume close. Send it. Don’t wait for extra approval. 3. **Use their timezone.** Make it easy. Avoid time zone back-and-forth. 4. **Control the frame.** You’re not asking for a favor - you’re offering help. 5. **Act fast.** Confirm within 10 minutes max. The window closes quickly. --- ## **Scripts You Can Use Today** --- **✅ Confirming Interest CTA** > “Can walk you through how we did it. > > > Free Tue 2pm or Wed 10am - either work for you?” > --- **✅ Booking Confirmation Message** > “Booked you in for Wednesday at 10am (your time). > > > You’ll have received the calendar invite. Looking forward to it.” > --- **✅ Vague Reply Response** > “Great, penciled in Wednesday 10am. > > > If that doesn’t work, just reply and choose: > > - Tue 2pm > - Thu 11am > > Otherwise see you then!” > --- **✅ CTA After Sending Info** > “Are you free Tue 3pm or Wed 11am and I can show you [outcome/mechanism as a result of info sent].” > --- ## **Summary** - Never send your booking link. - Offer 2 call times. - Assume the close once confirmed, then book it yourself. - Vague reply? Take control. Propose default + backups. - Your job isn’t to beg for the call, it’s to lead them to it. ← Previous [Know What to Say](/resources/know-what-to-say-241f6d94771f80ad9e3cdac600d3cecc) Next → [Delegate Your Inbox](Delegate%20Your%20Inbox%20(Inbox%20Manager%20Training)%20241f6d94771f8020b623f75e5f4acfe5.md) On this page

# How to Qualify Replies in 10 Seconds # **How to Qualify Replies in 10 Seconds** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This page is not about sales qualification. It’s about **triage**. You’ve got a reply. Is it worth responding? That’s all you need to decide right now. Fast, with confidence, without stalling.
## **What This Page Will Help You Do** --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Spot ICP-fit replies using the email alone no research rabbit hole ✅ Decide in under 10 seconds whether to reply or ignore ✅ Know when to check their site and exactly what to look for ✅ Build a personal “green = go” triage rule you can later hand off to an Inbox Manager --- ## **Why This Matters** --- Most $5K/month founders waste replies by overthinking them. They pause, hesitate, dig too deep, and end up replying late — or not at all. That means lost deals, lost momentum, and inbox anxiety. You’re not trying to guess their lifetime value. You’re deciding if they’re **worth a reply.** This is about speed. Directional confidence. Removing friction. --- ## **Step 1: Scan What’s Right In Front Of You (≤10 Seconds)** --- Before you even think about looking at their site, check the **reply itself** for buying signals. **Signals to look for:**
- **Email domain:** `@scaledgrowthmedia.com` > `@gmail.com` - **Sender name:** “Sam” > “Support” or “Team” - **Signature:** “Founder” / “CMO” = ✅ - **Tone:** Open language like “How does it work?” = ✅ - **DM signals:** Mentions of “my team”, “our clients”, “we help…”
If you see **at least one clear ICP signal**, **just reply.** You can always qualify further in the next message. ## **Step 2: If You’re Still Unsure, Scan Their Site (30 Seconds Max)** --- If the reply feels ambiguous, do a **quick site check**. What to look for:
✅ **Niche match** (e.g. SaaS) ✅ **Service alignment** (e.g. Paid Ads) ✅ **Proof** (case studies) **Then decide:** - ✅ Looks solid? → Reply. - ❌ Looks bad? → Ignore or reply with downsell/explanation why can’t help - 🟡 Still unsure? → Send a simple clarifying message: “Hey [Name], appreciate the reply, before I send more info, just checking: Are you working with SaaS clients right now?” > Let them qualify themselves. You don’t need to dig further. >
--- ## **Step 3: Write Your Rule (This Is Your Triage SOP)** ---
This is what you’ll later hand off to your Inbox Manager. But for now, write it for you so you stop hesitating and start moving.
### Example (for lead gen → Paid Ads Agencies Targeting SaaS): - ✅ Green = Branded domain, mentions SaaS, Reply from Founder/CMO - 🟡 Yellow = No niche or role clarity → scan site or ask - ❌ Red = Gmail, local SEO, no team, no niche → ignore That’s it. --- ## **Real Example Breakdown** --- > “Hey, might be open to lead gen, how does it work?” > > > **Email:** *sam@scaledgrowthmedia.com* > > **Signature:** “Co-Founder | Scaled Growth” > > **Site:** > > - “We help SaaS brands grow with YouTube Ads” ✅ > - Case study: +$185K ARR ✅ > - Team of 4 ✅ → ✅ **Total time: 20 seconds** → Reply + Book CTA --- > “Sure — send info.” > > > Email: *info@rankmymap.com* > > No signature > > Site: Local SEO for restaurants > → ❌ **Total time: 10 seconds** → Ignore or refer to someone in the group targeting SEO --- ## **Summary** --- From now on: - You’ll qualify replies in under 10 seconds - You’ll stop overthinking and start acting - You’ll build a simple green/yellow/red rule to filter fast and eventually hand off to someone else
❓If you’re still unsure, drop the reply in the group and we’ll triage it with you in 30 seconds.

# Know What to Say # Know What to Say ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** If a lead doesn't book a call, it's not because they weren’t interested. They replied, so they were. They didn’t book because your message didn’t make the next step clear. This page gives you the exact replies to use for every type of response. So you can reply quickly, book more calls, and always know what to say next.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Identify what kind of reply just came in (and what it *really* means) ✅ Know what the prospect actually wants from you ✅ Use the right message to either book the call or cleanly exit ✅ Build reply speed, confidence, and repeatability (zero guesswork) ## Why Most Founders Get This Wrong --- - **They try to convince.** Overexplaining when the lead just wants clarity. - **They use calendar links.** Booking links feel cold unless requested. - **They treat every reply the same.** No filtering = wasted time. - **They assume credibility.** Prospect doesn’t deem you credible yet. ## 1. Reply Response Map --- All replies you get will fall into one of these **five categories.**
This table breaks down: - What they *really* mean - What they actually want - What your reply should do
### Reply Response Map | **Reply Type** | **What it *really* means** | **What they want** | **Your reply goal** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | ❌ Not Interested | “No thanks” or “Remove me” | To be left alone | Exit cleanly. Don’t push. | | 🚫 Soft No | “Not a priority” or “Maybe later” | To avoid without hard rejection | Re-engage. Ask permission to follow up. | | 📊 Evaluating | “Send info” | Clarity, relevance, proof | Share proof → Book the call. | | ❓ Skeptical | “How are you different?” or “Sounds like X” | Contrast, credibility | Reframe their assumption → book call. | | ✅ Interested | “This looks good” or “How does it work?” | A clear next step | Get the call booked fast. | ## 2. Confusing Replies: What They Really Mean ---
For edge cases that don’t fit the 5 types until you learn to see the patterns)
--- ### 1. “We already use someone for this” - **Type:** 📊 Evaluating - **What they want:** To avoid wasting time unless the switch is worth it - **Your goal:** Offer a cost and service comparison - **Reply tip:** Don’t ask if they’re happy. Highlight how they’ve not done a side-by-side comparison --- ### 2. “What’s the price?” - **Type:** ✅ Interested or 📊 Evaluating - **What they want:** To judge if it’s worth their time - **Your goal:** Tease range only *if required*, book call to explain value - **Reply tip:** If for example you’re selling lead gen, you could say: > ”Pricing depends on [variables] but we guarantee to beat your CAC by X%” > --- ### 3. “No budget right now” - **Type:** 🚫 Soft No - **What they want:** Exit without confrontation - **Your goal:** Ask when to re-engage, offer non-pushy ROI context - **Reply tip:** Acknowledge budget + permission to follow up later --- ### 5. “Reach out in 3 months” - **Type:** 🚫 Soft No - **What they want:** Delay without full rejection - **Your goal:** Lock in future follow-up + share proof - **Reply tip:** Confirm re-engagement and drop value asset --- ### 6. “Forwarded to [Another Contact]” - **Type:** ✅ Interested (new lead) - **What they want:** You to handle the handoff - **Your goal:** Win over the new contact from scratch - **Reply tip:** Mention who referred you, relaunch with relevance --- ## 3. Real Examples (Per Type) --- Use these as frameworks, not scripts. You're thinking like a founder now. Your job is to think in frameworks and systems, not templates. --- ### ❌ Not Interested **Reply:** “Not interested” **Framework:** > “All good, you won’t hear from us again. Appreciate the response.” > **Why:** Protects deliverability and maintains brand integrity. On rare occasions, these turn into leads later, but only if you exit cleanly now. --- ### 🚫 Soft No **Reply:** “Not a priority right now” or “Circle back in Q4” **Framework:** > “Totally understand, is this something that might be a priority in 3 months? If so, we’ll check back in and send you [value asset] then.” > **Why:** Polite persistence + re-engagement permission = your next 90-day pipeline. --- ### 📊 Evaluating **Reply:** “Already using someone / Can you send more info?” **Framework:** > “Makes sense. When was the last time you did a cost and service comparison? > > > Helped [Competitor Name] switch from [Old Provider] → 2.3x leads in 6 weeks. > > Can walk you through how we did it. Free Tue 2pm or Wed 11am?” > **Why:** This is a value gap play. Show them there's a better way. --- ### ❓ Skeptical **Reply:** “How are you different from X?” **Framework:** > “Fair question. Most [buyer type] we work with felt the same. > > > What changed was seeing this: [insert single powerful case study or metric] > > Can show you what we did → Tue 10am or Wed 4pm?” > **Why:** Don’t argue. Just reframe their assumption with proof and clarity. --- ### ✅ Interested **Reply:** “This looks good, how does it work?” **Framework:** > “Quick overview: we handle [X] so you can [Y]. > > > Helped [Case Study] go from [start point] → [end result] in 45 days. > > Can walk you through the setup. Free Tue 3pm or Wed 10am?” > **Why:** Minimise delay. Close that interest gap *now*, before it cools. --- ## 4. Rules for Replying **Follow these every time. Train your inbox team to live by them.** --- 1. **No booking links.** Always offer 2 time slots. Booking links feel cold and get ignored. 2. **Reply fast — <5 min target.** Speed shows seriousness. Prospects mirror your urgency. 3. **Use their language.** Mirror their words back. 4. **Filter before replying.** Not every reply is worth a 300-word essay. Qualify → then respond. 5. **Never convince.** Your reply’s job isn’t to explain the entire service. Just enough proof to *earn the call*. 6. **Be clear, not clever.** End every reply with a direct next step. 7. **You’re equals.** Whether it’s a $1B CEO or a solo founder, reply with clarity, not desperation. --- ## Summary --- Review this weekly. Train your VA or inbox manager on this logic. Inbox mastery = reply velocity + response clarity + lead intent matching. ← Previous [How to Qualify Replies in 10 Seconds](/resources/how-to-qualify-replies-in-10-seconds-241f6d94771f802d83d8ed3c6f27ee24) Next → [Get The Call Booked](Get%20the%20Call%20Booked%20(CTA%20+%20Confirmation)%20241f6d94771f80b6846de8389e093bfd.md) On this page

# Never Miss a Reply (Automation) # **Never Miss a Reply (Automation)** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** A reply you don’t see is a reply you can’t book. This is the first revenue leak: founders *get* responses but miss them, check late, or forget to reply. This page fixes that.
## What This Page Will Help You Do By the end of this page, you will: ✅ Get notified in Slack the second you get a reply ✅ React faster than 95% of your market ✅ Eliminate the “I missed that reply” excuse forever ✅ Set your team or Inbox Manager up to respond fast without you ## Why You Should Automate This --- Speed = perceived value. Most founders still check inbox manually. That’s why they reply 12 hours late… if at all. But the fastest responder usually gets the call; not the smartest, not the best.
**In fact. It’s proven that those that respond to interested replies within 5-minutes will literally 100x their chances of converting.**
You’ve already done the hard work getting the reply. Now you need to see it instantly and act. Every hour you delay: - The lead forgets who you are - Their problem feels less urgent - They take a call with someone else Automation stops that slide before it starts. --- ## How to Automate (Based On Your Tool) --- ### Instantly --- Set up a Slack notification for every reply inside Instantly using their native integrations. → Takes 90 seconds. [https://www.loom.com/share/404d30fb504f4a36b454311420dcd711?sid=ccf1d9be-cb6b-436c-ad05-73890d0dcc58](https://www.loom.com/share/404d30fb504f4a36b454311420dcd711?sid=ccf1d9be-cb6b-436c-ad05-73890d0dcc58) ### Smartlead --- Use Smartlead’s integration to push every reply to Slack → Takes 90 seconds. [https://www.loom.com/share/46bbebb2a59e44b19be46ab831659ac2?sid=a261faf0-4d21-4625-bc7b-1fcd99f484e1](https://www.loom.com/share/46bbebb2a59e44b19be46ab831659ac2?sid=a261faf0-4d21-4625-bc7b-1fcd99f484e1) --- ## Summary --- From now on: - Every reply will trigger an instant alert. - You (or your team) will never miss a lead again. - Reaction time drops to <15 minutes the minimum bar for founders who want to close more deals **💡 Tip:** Add a ✅ reaction in Slack when a reply is handled. That way you/your team always know what’s been responded to and what hasn’t. ← Previous [Master Your Inbox](../Master%20Your%20Inbox%20(Turn%20Replies%20Into%20Booked%20Calls)%20241f6d94771f8001a2eecc078dc738ff.md) Next → [Qualify Replies in 10 Seconds](/resources/how-to-qualify-replies-in-10-seconds-241f6d94771f802d83d8ed3c6f27ee24) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/inbox-.content:
# Lead Nurturing # Lead Nurturing ---
**🚨QUICK NOTE:** Lead Nurturing = automated follow-up that builds trust and books calls without you doing everything manually and for leads that you couldn’t initially re-engage or aren’t ready right now.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know **exactly when** to start nurturing ✅ Install a **long-term automated sequence** that reactivates leads ✅ Learn what **effective nurture emails** look like ✅ Keep your pipeline warm without manual follow-ups or guessing ✅ Have a set-it-and-forget-it nurture campaign that brings leads back months later ## 1. What Leads Get Nurtured? --- Let’s be crystal clear: **Nurture ≠ Follow-Up.** Follow-up is about getting a reply. **Nurture is what you do when replies stop.** This is **not** about chasing. It’s about staying visible, useful, and trusted **without annoying them**. ### 1a. When Does Nurture Start? --- After you’ve: - Tried to re-engage - Gave them time to consider or get back to you And still got… → Silence → “Not right now” → “Maybe Q4” → “This looks great, just not ready yet” These aren’t dead leads. They’re **not ready leads.** And they will come back if you play the long game right.
**Reminder:** Only 3% of your target market is ready to buy at any one time 41% will be ready to buy in the next 3-12 months.
--- ### 1b. Common Scenarios That Call for Nurture --- Here’s exactly when to push a lead into your nurture campaign: - ✅ **Ghosted after a positive call** You followed up. Nothing. Couldn’t get them back. → Push into nurture and let time + value do the work. - ✅ **Replied but said “maybe later”** E.g. “Reach out in Q4,” “circle back in a few months” → They don’t want to be chased—but they’re not lost. Stay on their radar. - ✅ **Said yes, then disappeared** They asked for more info, sounded keen… then vanished. → You followed up, but never heard back. Stop chasing. Start nurturing. - ✅ **Re-engagement failed** You tried reviving them but they didn’t reply. → Move them into the nurture campaign ---
This is where most founders go wrong. They either **keep chasing** (and kill trust) Or they **do nothing** (and lose the lead forever) Nurture is your third path: **High-leverage. Low effort. Long-term compounding.** Want to move on to the “Nurturing Campaign” setup?
## **2. The Lead Nurturing Campaign Strategy** --- When someone says “not yet” or ghosts you completely, you need a **nurturing machine** that keeps delivering value **without you lifting a finger**.
❌ No manual follow-ups ✅ No awkward chasing ✅ No falling off their radar
### 2a. The Structure (Simple + Long-Term) ---
- **12 Emails** - **1 Email Per Month** - **30 Days Between Emails** - **All Pre-Written + Pre-Scheduled** - **Zero Sales Pressure**
This is NOT a sales campaign. It’s a **slow-drip trust builder**. --- ### 2b. How to Set It Up 1. **Create a New Campaign** Inside your ESP (e.g. Instantly, Smartlead, Bison), create a nurture campaign with 12 emails. 2. **Push Leads In Manually** After follow-up fails or they say “not now,” drop them into this sequence. 3. **Let It Run** You don’t need to touch it again. Every month, they get something useful and you stay top of mind.
This is your **set-and-forget engine** for closing leads 3, 6, even 12 months after your first convo.
## 3. What Should the Emails Look Like? --- You’re probably thinking….. **“12 emails!? Wtf am I going to say?”** Fair question.
So here’s the framework for your nurture sequence. It should make them **feel like they’re learning from you for free**… until the day they realise they’d rather just pay you.
### What Each Email Should Do: --- - Deliver one useful insight or shift in thinking - Show you understand their world - Stay short, relevant, and non-salesy - Build quiet pressure to re-engage ## 12 Monthly Email Angles (1 per month) ---
ℹ️ Use these as the insights/angles you need to write each email in the sequence and plug them into your new nurturing campaign.
--- ### **📅 Month 1 – Market POV** ---
→ Call out a key trend and what it means for them.
### **📅 Month 2 – Small Tactical Win** ---
→ Share a fast, useful idea they could apply today.
### **📅 Month 3 – Results Story** ---
→ “Client was stuck on X → now they’re at Y.” Keep it short and real.
### **📅  Month 4 – Myth Bust** ---
→ Call out what most people get wrong and why it costs them.
### **📅 Month 5 – Simple Framework** ---
→ Break down how you approach a common challenge. 3–5 bullets.
### **📅 Month 6 – Industry Angle** ---
→ Speak directly to one vertical you serve. Prove relevance.
### **📅 Month 7 – Bonus Value** ---
→ Share something useful *outside* your offer (tool, article, playbook).
### **📅 Month 8 – Challenge the Delay** ---
→ Show what delaying the fix is actually costing them.
### **📅 Month 9 – Hard Lesson** ---
→ Real-world friction you hit (or a client did) and the fix.
### **📅 Month 10 – Cost of Inaction** ---
→ Lay out what happens when this stays broken.
### **📅 Month 11 – Momentum Proof** ---
→ Share a new win, stat, or campaign insight from the last 30 days.
### **📅 Month 12 – Light Re-Invite** ---
→ “Still want to fix X? Let me know if now’s a better time.”
## 4. What Happens If They Don’t Respond? --- This is where most founders overthink. You’ll start wondering:
“Should I chase them again?” “Should I try another angle?” “What if they never reply?”
Here’s the truth: **If they don’t respond, that’s okay.** You stayed relevant. You delivered value. You didn’t pester. You did what 99% of founders don’t: → **You built long-term brand equity inside their inbox.** Your name is now tied to helpful, high-signal insights. They’ll remember that when the timing is right. --- ## 7. Action Plan: Do This Now --- Don’t overthink it. Don’t wait. Build the system once and let it run for years. Here’s your 24h playbook: --- ### ✅ Step 1: Create a New Nurture Campaign → Load up 12 monthly emails using the angles above → 30 days apart, no pitch, just value --- ### ✅ Step 2: Identify Leads to Push In → Anyone who ghosted after call/proposal → Anyone who said “not now” → Anyone who didn’t re-engage Use your pipeline tracker or CRM to filter for these. --- ### ✅ Step 3: Push Them In + Move On → Drop those leads into the nurture flow → Don’t obsess over replies → Let the machine work ---
1-2 hours of setup = clients booked 3, 6, or 12 months from now.

# Re-Engaging Leads # Re-Engaging Leads ---
**🚨QUICK NOTE:** Re-engagement = leads who showed buying intent but went cold. This page helps you re-engage them.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Identify the exact leads who should be re-engaged ✅ Know how to build and run a re-engagement campaign ✅ Re-engage warm leads from the last 3–6 months and turn them into booked calls --- ## Why Re-Engage? --- Most founders treat follow-ups like an afterthought. But the real compounding revenue lives in leads you’ve already paid for. Let’s say:
- You had 30 warm leads last month - 15 booked a call - 2 clients closed
That means:
- 13 booked but didn’t close - 15 never booked at all - Some were “not now”. Not “never”
And most founders… never bother. That’s $10K–$30K/month in future MRR left on the table. --- ## What Leads Should Be Re-Engaged? --- Use your **Closing Clients Pipeline Tracker** to filter leads who: - ✅ Replied but never booked - ✅ Booked a call but didn’t show - ✅ Got a proposal but went cold - ✅ Said “not now” in the last 3–6 months
**If your Airtable tracker isn’t set up yet**, go back to [**Install Your Pipeline Tracker (Walkthrough)**](../Track%20and%20Manage%20Your%20Pipeline/Install%20Your%20Pipeline%20Tracker%20(Walkthrough)%20241f6d94771f8083bf72c0f9a3512453.md) **You can’t re-engage what you’re not tracking.**
--- ## When to Run Re-Engagement Campaigns --- **Do this first.**
Open your tracker and once you’ve filled in past data: - Filter leads based on the criteria above - Set the date range to **30–180 days ago** - Push those leads into your re-engagement campaign (next)
This will allow you to identify the leads needed to be re-engaged. Then you want to implement this monthly.
**On the 1st of every month:** - Filter your tracker based on the above criteria for the past 30 days - Push them into your re-engagement campaign
--- ## How to Build a Re-Engagement Campaign ---
This is **not** a one-off follow-up. It’s a short campaign to re-warm leads and turn them into calls. Here’s the sequence we recommend:
### Re-Engagement Flow: 4 emails over 3 weeks + optional call | **Step** | **Day** | **Action** | | --- | --- | --- | | Email 1 | Day 1 | Quick re-intro | | Email 2 | Day 5 | Proof drop or case study | | Email 3 | Day 9 | “Saw this, thought of you” style nudge | | Email 4 | Day 14 | Stronger CTA + time-sensitive reason | | Optional Call | Day 18–20 | Call them if high-fit lead or proposal was sent |
⚙️ **How to Push Leads In** You/your team can do this manually in Instantly or Smartlead by tagging and uploading leads into a re-engagement campaign. Or if you’re technical enough you could automate this using your tools API.
## What to Do After the Campaign --- Once the sequence finishes: - **If they reply → qualify and book them** - **If no reply → push them into your Nurture campaign** (next section) --- ## Summary --- Re-engagement is how you: - Stop leads leaking out of your pipeline - Turn “not now” into “booked this week” - Recover warm leads without spending more on new ones You don’t need to be constantly chasing new leads. You need to work the one you already have. ← Previous [Why Re-Engagement + Nurturing Matter](/resources/why-re-engagement-nurturing-matter-241f6d94771f80ecb1f8e0a41eecfa7e) Next → [Lead Nurturing](/resources/lead-nurturing-241f6d94771f8052b7cfd0107ff56cdc) On this page

# Why Re-Engagement + Nurturing Matter # Why Re-Engagement + Nurturing Matter ---
**🚨QUICK NOTE:** Re-engagement = leads who showed buying intent but went cold Nurturing = leads who weren’t ready *yet* but will be in 3–12 months Most founders ignore both.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Understand the two lead types most founders neglect (and why that’s costing you deals) ✅ See exactly why follow-up isn’t just polite, it’s how you compound *revenue* ✅ Recognise how re-engagement + nurturing drive future MRR without chasing more leads ## Why Re-Engagement + Nurturing Matter --- Most founders obsess over getting new leads. But that’s not where most of your *next clients* are hiding. Let’s look at the math:
- You got 30 warm leads last month - 15 booked a call - You closed 2
That means:
- 13 leads showed intent but didn’t close - 15 leads never booked, but were warm enough to reply - Some of those 30 just weren’t ready yet, not a “no,” just “not now”
Most founders never speak to them again. That’s $10K, $20K, $30K/month in compounding future MRR left on the table. --- ## The Biggest Leak in Your Pipeline ---
Only ~3% of your market is ready to buy right now. But **41% will be ready in 3–12 months**.
If you ghost them, someone else will close them. If you stay top of mind, you can win them *without new effort*. This is the secret to compounding:
New leads fill your pipeline. **Follow-up compounds your MRR.**
Most $5K founders treat outbound like fishing. The smart ones treat it like farming. --- ## Summary --- There are two silent revenue streams you’re probably ignoring: - **Re-engagement**: leads who were hot but dropped off - **Nurturing**: leads who will be hot if you stay visible You already paid to generate these leads. You don’t need to chase more *new* leads, you need to engage the ones who showed interest. ← Previous [Re-Engage + Nurture](/resources/re-engage-nurture-leads-241f6d94771f80608c7cda3f6479b520) Next → [Re-Engaging Leads](/resources/re-engaging-leads-241f6d94771f80f8aae1dd9e3a6df301) On this page

# Forecast Your Growth # Forecast Your Growth ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Forecasting is not guessing. Guessing is “hope we close 3 deals this month.” Forecasting is “we’ll close 3 if we improve these 2 metrics by X.” This section turns your tracker into a growth-planning tool
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Forecast how much revenue you'll close in the next 30–60 days using real conversion data ✅ Spot the bottlenecks limiting growth and know what to fix ✅ Have a clear path to finally hit $30K/month ## What Most Founders Get Wrong --- **❌ They only look backward.** They know how last month went, but don’t know how this or next month will go **❌ They set goals without math.** Saying “I want 5 clients this month” means nothing if they don’t know how. **❌ They don’t link actions to revenue.** They increase sending or pitch harder, but aren’t fixing the metric that's limiting growth. ## How Forecasting Works --- You already track:
- Positive Replies - Booked Calls - Show-Up Rate - Qualified % - Proposal Sent - Deals Won / Lost - Proposal → Close Rate - Sales Cycle
To forecast, we work backwards:
Target Revenue ➝ Deals Needed ➝ Qualified Calls ➝ Booked Calls ➝ Positive Replies ➝ Emails to Send
Same logic as in “Know Your Numbers” but now, the pipeline proves where friction is. ## Use the Outbound Math Calculator To Forecast ---
If you've gone through the **Know Your Numbers** page, this tool will look familiar. But the use case here is different. You're not just observing past performance, you're planning future revenue. If you already know your true close rate (which you’ll track in your **Pipeline Tracker**), you don’t need to worry about tracking “proposals sent vs close” etc here. That’s handled in the Airtable dashboard. This calculator just helps you forecast how many leads → calls → clients you need.
## **Use the Calculator Now** # 👉 [**Open the Outbound Math Calculator**](https://www.closingclientsgroup.com/outbound-math-calculator) ![Screenshot 2025-07-22 at 09.09.16.png](../../Client%20Acquisition%20Engine/Build%20the%20Campaign%20Engine%20That%20Books%20Calls/Know%20Your%20Numbers/Screenshot_2025-07-22_at_09.09.16.png) - Plug in your data - Pick your monthly client goal - It will show how many leads and inboxes you need to hit it This is how you scale --- ## Example: From $5K → $30K/month ---
Let’s say Sam’s doing $5K/month and wants to grow to $30K/month. He sells a $3K/month offer, so he needs **10 clients**.
He currently:
- Closes 25% of sales calls - Gets 80% show-up rate - Needs ~500 leads to generate 1 booked call
With the calculator, he inputs:
- 10 client goal - 25% close rate - 80% show-up rate - 500 leads → call ratio
🔢 Result:
- He needs ~50 booked calls - Which requires 25,000 leads/month - Which equals 1,250 new leads/day (over 20 days) - With a 2-email sequence and 25 emails/inbox/day ➝ he needs ~40 inboxes
Sam now knows *exactly* what needs to improve:
- If he improves his close rate to 33%, he can hit $30K with only 7–8 clients - If he fixes targeting, reducing leads-to-call ratio to 250:1, he cuts lead volume in half
--- ## Summary --- Forecasting isn’t magic. It’s just subtraction and division. You now: - Know the key metrics that move revenue - Can plan lead volume based on your real funnel math - Understand where to focus to grow ← Previous [Install Your Pipeline Tracker](Install%20Your%20Pipeline%20Tracker%20(Walkthrough)%20241f6d94771f8083bf72c0f9a3512453.md) Next → [Track and manage Your Pipeline](/resources/track-and-manage-your-pipeline-241f6d94771f803fb928fb899f374be7) On this page

# What You Should Track And Why # **What You Should Track And Why** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You’ve already tracked the campaign side: sends → reply → calls from here [Know Your Numbers](/resources/know-your-numbers-23bf6d94771f8027883ddbeb0b217f8e) Now you need to track what happens after the campaign stage If you don’t monitor calls booked, shows, qualified, close rates etc like you do reply %, you’ll have no clue why you're stuck and won’t be able to scale.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ See where deals stall between reply and revenue ✅ Track only the metrics that actually drive cash ✅ Spot bottlenecks instantly so you know what to fix ✅ Install the same pipeline logic top closers use ## What Most Founders Get Wrong --- - **They stop at booked calls.** You’re celebrating a calendar invite while your revenue engine is leaking at every seam. - **They confuse activity with progress.** If you're not tracking shows, proposals, and closes you’re just guessing. - **They have no deal visibility.** Meaning they don’t know what to follow up with, what got lost and are blind. --- ## What You Should Track (The Pipeline Core Metrics) | **Stage** | **Metric** | | --- | --- | | **Top of Pipeline** | Positive Replies (Total) | | **Booking** | Booked Calls | | **Booking Efficiency** | Booked Calls ÷ Positive Replies | | **Show-Up** | Show-Up Rate % | | **No-Show** | No-Show % | | **Qualification** | Qualified Calls % | | **Proposal** | Proposals Sent | | **Sales** | Deals Won / Lost | | **Call Performance** | Close Rate % | | **Conversions** | Proposal → Close Rate % | | **Cycle** | Sales-Cycle Length | ## Why These Metrics Matter --- - **Positive Replies** = size of your actual deal pipeline - **Booked Call %** = are inbox managers booking or bottlenecking? - **Show-Up Rate** = do prospects believe you solve the right problem? - **Qualified Calls %** = how clean is your targeting? - **Proposals Sent** = is your discovery call creating desire + urgency? - **Deals Won / Lost** = is your offer converting? - **Proposal → Close %** = how persuasive is your follow-up + close? - **Sales-Cycle Length** = how long until you get paid? --- ## Summary --- You’ve already made outbound math predictable. Now we’re making revenue math predictable. We know your next thought will be “ok but how do I track this without a CRM?” and you’ll see in the next section that we’re one-step ahead 😉 ← Previous [Track and Manage Your Pipeline](/resources/track-and-manage-your-pipeline-241f6d94771f803fb928fb899f374be7) Next → [Install Your Pipeline Tracker](Install%20Your%20Pipeline%20Tracker%20(Walkthrough)%20241f6d94771f8083bf72c0f9a3512453.md) On this page

# $30K Sales Mentality # $30K Sales Mentality ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This system exists to change the way you think about sales from convincing to diagnosing.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Know why you should stop trying to persuade prospects ✅ What you should do instead ✅ Rewire your view of sales ✅ Understand how belief → emotion → trust → close works ✅ Start thinking like a buyer, not a seller --- ## What Most Founders Get Wrong --- They believe:
“Sales = say the right thing and get them to say yes.”
So they: - Try to convince - Scramble to handle objections - Sound desperate - Are too afriad to ask the right questions because they think it will fuck up the sale **Example using Lead Gen Services:**
You’re having a conversation with a potential client. **You ask:** “Great so what problem are you trying to solve” **They say:** ”Well we’re just looking really, we already have a sales team and they’re doing really good but we can always do with more leads right?” **The average founder, who’s trying to sell, trying to convince will say:** ”Ha of course! Well that’s great you’ve already got a sales team and of course we can always do with more leads and we can definitely help you with that!” I bet you’re even thinking..that’s exactly what I’d say…in fact I have said that. Let me tell you what a good sales person would say. Then read back through the above example and see how stupid it is to say you can help when you don’t know if you can yet, how desperate it sounds and how it’s just posturing to convince. **A good person would respond:** ”Ok great! Why not just hire more sales people?” **Why?** Because they’re trying to diagnose and truly understand. Not persuade.
--- ## The $30K Switch: Sales = Diagnose, Don’t Persuade ---
**You should NOT be trying to SELL. Until you can CONFIRM you understand their problem thoroughly and you’re the BEST option.**
If you show up trying to win the sale, you lose control. You:
- Talk too much - Overpromise - Chase - Collapse on price
But if you show up to diagnose:
- You ask real questions - You qualify faster - You control the pace - You can walk away which makes you more trusted
> Objections are scary when you’re trying to convince. But not when you know their exact problem and KNOW you’re the best solution. > --- ## Think Like a Buyer --- Buyers don’t want to be convinced. They want to feel understood. They’re not wondering:
> “Are they good enough to sell me on this?” >
They’re asking:
> “Do they get what I’m trying to solve?” ”Have they already done this for someone like me?” > > > “Do I trust that they can actually do this?” > > “Do they believe in their own offer?” >
--- ## The Good → Great → Best Sales Model | Level | What You Sell | Outcome | | --- | --- | --- | | Good | Features + Benefits | “Sounds cool, maybe later.” | | Great | Outcomes | “This could work, send more info.” | | Best | Emotion → Outcome → Logic | “This is exactly what I need. Let’s go.” |
The easiest way to win is making them feel understood, showing you know their situation, know the solution and can solve it for them.
--- ## Belief = Frame Control --- Not selling, removes pressure. Understanding you’re diagnosing lets you stay curious. Selling = panic → pitch → fumble --- ### Example On How To Flip The Frame | Buyer Says | You Say (Frame Flip) | | --- | --- | | “We’ve tried something like this.” | “Nice, what went wrong with it?” | | “We have a team doing this now.” | “Then what’s missing that brought you here?” | | “It’s expensive.” | “Compared to what?” | | “Can you send me something after?” | “Sure, just so I send the right thing, what’s unclear now?” | **You’re not fighting to sell.** **You’re just looking for clarity.** --- ## Emotion First, Then Logic --- Every buyer has 3 brains: 1. **Emotional** → “I want this solved” 2. **Rational** → “Does this make sense?” 3. **Status** → “Do I look smart saying yes?” ![Untitled design (2).png]($30K%20Sales%20Mentality/Untitled_design_(2).png) If you lead with logic, they stay skeptical. If you lead with calm emotion and back it with proof, they will close themselves.
> “I’ve done this 14 times with people in your exact spot. > > > We can fix this but only if what you’re saying is actually the real bottleneck. Mind if I ask a few questions?” >
That’s the $30K/Month approach to selling. --- ## Exercise: Rehearse the Reversal ---
Do this now 👇
**Write down 3 objections that make you flinch.** → What do you normally say? → What after reading this would you now say instead? --- ## Summary --- STOP trying to persuade. START diagnosing the problems/goals. You need to:
- Know your buyer - Believe in your offer - Stay calm under pressure - Ask better questions than they’ve ever been asked
That’s what closes deals. ← Previous [Sales](/resources/sales-241f6d94771f80b4ba2fc227c85493c3) Next → [Diagnose Your Sales Bottleneck](/resources/diagnose-your-sales-bottleneck-245f6d94771f8019997ae0519d106216) On this page

# Diagnose Your Sales Bottleneck # Diagnose Your Sales Bottleneck ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This page exists to help you spot the things you're doing that you shouldn’t be.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you’ll: ✅ Spot the behaviours that kill your calls before they even start ✅ Understand ***why*** you’re not closing — it’s not your price or your leads ## Diagnose What’s Actually Breaking Your Calls ---
Count each box that applies to you, to get your score.
### **SELF-AUDIT** --- ### 🧠 Mindset + Belief ☐ I go into calls trying to **persuade**, not **diagnose** ☐ I say “we can help” too fast, even when I’m unsure we can help ☐ I avoid asking hard questions in case it kills the deal ☐ I price based on what I *think they can afford*, not my deal structure. --- ### 🎯 Process + Posture ☐ I don’t have a clear “**price drop**” moment ☐ I change my offer mid-call to try and save the sale ☐ I talk for more than **70% of the call** ☐ I panic or over-talk when they go quiet ☐ I still don’t know **why most leads actually booked the call** ☐ I never present a **clear** next step for what we should do after the call --- ### 🧮 Your Score --- - **0–2 boxes:** You’re probably doing better than you think. Next step is tightening control - **3–6 boxes:** You’re in the danger zone, no system, just instincts. - **7–10 boxes:** You’re the reason your offer won’t scale. ## What Happens Next --- Now you know what you’re doing wrong. We will show you what you should be doing instead. ← Previous [$30K Sales Mentality](/resources/30k-sales-mentality-245f6d94771f80578df0d2bc13f35bf1) Next → [Pre-Call: Control the Call](/resources/pre-call-control-the-call-before-it-starts-245f6d94771f80ea8674e9a84a5d9734) On this page

# Map Out Your Sales Process # **Map Out Your Sales Process** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This isn’t about downloading a plug-and-play template. This is about YOU building a process that reflects how your business actually operates. If you want a system that actually works and scales, it needs to be built. Not borrowed. This page will help you build yours properly.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand the core components your sales process must include ✅ Know how to structure it around your own lead flow, offer, and ops ✅ See a complete sales process visualised (example diagram) ✅ Be ready to map your own in Miro, Whimsical, or Notion --- ## What Your Sales Process Should Cover ---
A complete sales process maps out the **entire journey from stranger to signed client**.
### Your Process Should Include: --- ### **1. Lead Generation** ---
How prospects find you and enter your system Cold outbound, referrals, inbound, paid ads, etc.
### **2. Lead Qualification** ---
How you determine who’s worth your time Research, scoring, filters, TAM, intent
### **3. Lead Nurturing** ---
How you move prospects toward sales conversations Re-engagement, warm-up sequences, content, reminders
### **4 Sales Conversations** ---
How you conduct your sales calls and handle outcomes One-call close? Two-call? Pre-call show up process?
### **5. Closing & Contracts** ---
How you turn “yes” into paid client Proposal, agreement, invoice, payment, handover
### **6. Onboarding** ---
How new clients transition to delivery Kickoff call, Slack invite, assets, delivery timelines
--- ### **Each stage should have:**
✅ Clear entry and exit criteria ✅ Defined actions and responsibilities ✅ Measurable outcomes and timeframes ✅ Documented workflows and templates
Otherwise, it’s not a system. --- ## Example: Sales Process Diagram --- This is an example we created for Closing Clients. Use it as a reference, but build your own to reflect your real-world flow. [https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVLufOkhk=/](https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVLufOkhk=/) --- ## How to Create Your Sales Process --- You can use tools like:
- **Miro** (recommended for drag-and-drop flow) - **Whimsical** (fast and simple) - **Notion** (for documentation and embeds)
--- ## Here’s how to structure it: --- 1. **Start With Entry Points**
Where do leads come in from? (Cold? Inbound? Referrals?)
2. **Mark Decision Points**
Are they qualified? Booked? Closed? Add key yes/no forks.
3. **Add Action Steps**
Emails, reminders, follow-ups, calls, proposals, invoices.
4. **Account for Every Outcome**
- No-shows - "Not ready yet" - “Send me something” - Won deals - Lost deals
5. **Simplify the First Version**
Your first draft should fit on one screen. Don’t try to build perfection.
--- ## What Makes a Good Sales Process? --- ✅ It’s mapped visually ✅ You know what happens at every stage ✅ Nothing falls through the cracks ✅ It can be handed off or delegated ✅ You can find and fix bottlenecks ✅ You can track real metrics across the funnel ← Previous [What A Sales Process Is + Why You Need One](/resources/what-a-sales-process-is-why-you-need-one-24af6d94771f8081add7cdbb34535343) Next → [The Feedback Loop - Continuous Improvement](/resources/the-feedback-loop-continuous-improvement-24af6d94771f8015a38de879b3b51b35) On this page
